title: Senior Business Development & Sales Manager
description_text:
About CREATEQ
We are a leading custom software company, partnering with clients across industries to design, build, and scale digital solutions. With engineering centers across Europe and diverse delivery expertise, we help businesses achieve faster time-to-market, higher quality, and long-term value. 
Our company's commitment to "where creativity meets technology" is the driving force behind our high-quality software engineering solutions. CREATEQ offers comprehensive services across all project phases, from ideation to software design & architecture and end-to-end delivery.
We are now looking for a Senior Business Development & Sales Manager to join our leadership team in Switzerland. 
This role is key to drive business growth with new and existing clients. 
Your Mission
As Senior Business Development & Sales Manager, you are responsible for winning new clients for CREATEQ's custom software engineering and near-shoring services. 
You own the entire commercial lifecycle from first contact to signed contract and work closely with delivery and engineering leadership to shape compelling, realistic and scalable solutions.
You build pipeline through a structured multi-channel go-to-market approach (outbound, inbound follow-up, referrals, partners and selected events) and act as the main commercial interface to prospects and clients.
This is a hands-on new business role with a strong entrepreneurial component and high autonomy. 
What you will do & key responsibilities
Acquire new clients (new logos) in Switzerland and in the European market
Build and manage a healthy, predictable sales pipeline using multi-channel outreach
Systematically activate your existing network into long-term, paying client relationships
Leverage your contacts and industry presence to open doors and build trust
Lead discovery conversations with C-level and senior decision-makers (CIO, CTO, Business Owners, Head of Digital, Product Owners, Head of Engineering)
Position CREATEQ’s software engineering expertise onsite and nearshoring model (Swiss account leadership, architects and analysts + European delivery) clearly and credibly
Shape proposals, commercial models and contracts together with delivery leadership
Negotiate and close long-term software engineering engagements (projects, modernization, operations, dedicated teams, staff augmentation)
Collaborate with marketing on positioning, campaigns, case studies and market presence
Represent CREATEQ externally with confidence and professionalism
Feed market insights back into strategy, offerings and positioning
What we are looking for :
Proven track record in B2B business development and sales in custom software industry
Strong understanding of software engineering, architectures, and service delivery models
Experience selling professional services, software engineering and nearshore / offshore distributed delivery models
Ability to independently open doors, qualify opportunities and close deals
Strong commercial judgement and structured sales approach
Comfortable working without a large sales team, but closely aligned with delivery and marketing
Excellent communication and presentation skills, able to engage stakeholders at all levels
Fluent in German and English
Based in Greater Zurich Area
Willingness to travel across target markets
Why Join CREATEQ?
High-impact role with direct influence on the company’s growth
Clear focus on new business with strong delivery backing
Entrepreneurial environment with short decision paths
Competitive compensation with performance-based upside
Hybrid working model 40/60 (office in Pfäffikon SZ, next to the train station)
Strong collaboration with nearshore delivery centres and partners across Europe
Ready to Build the Future with Us?
If you are a senior, credible sales professional who enjoys building business, shaping deals and creating long-term client relationships, we would love to hear from you.
Apply now and help shape the growth story of CREATEQ!
ai_core_responsibilities: The Senior Business Development & Sales Manager is responsible for acquiring new clients and managing the entire commercial lifecycle from first contact to signed contract. This role involves building a healthy sales pipeline and collaborating with delivery and engineering leadership to shape solutions.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a proven track record in B2B business development and sales within the custom software industry, along with a strong understanding of software engineering and service delivery models. Excellent communication skills and fluency in German and English are also required.

title: Strategy Project Manager
description_text:
Bauer Media Outdoor estate spans 12 European countries, helping advertisers reach millions of consumers through 110,000 Out-of-Home assets. With a diverse portfolio of public infrastructure solutions and services, and iconic locations, we connect brands and partners with audiences in creative and innovative ways.
Using data analytics and programmatic capabilities, we provide tailored advertising experiences and measurable insights that help our partners reach the right audiences, deliver impact and drive engagement.
We are a platform for brands and a platform for good. We are focused on delivering more impact for advertisers, more value for communities, inspiring social change, and less impact on the planet. Bauer Media Outdoors’s advertising opportunities are in everyday spaces where people live, work, and travel, providing a highly impactful platform to build brands.
About the role.. 
As Strategy Project Manager (Commercial), your role is to help turn strategy into action across Bauer Media Outdoor’s European and UK businesses.
Reporting to the Strategy Director (Europe & UK), you will support high-impact commercial initiatives that directly influence revenue growth, commercial effectiveness and product strategy. Sitting at the intersection of strategy, commercial execution and data-led decision making, you will bring structure, insight and momentum to priority projects across multiple markets.
You will work closely with senior commercial leaders, local market teams and cross-functional partners across Out-of-Home, Audio and Publishing. A key focus of the role will be supporting Project Orchestra, Bauer’s programme to align and integrate commercial capabilities across the Group, helping ensure initiatives are well-defined, insight-led and delivered effectively.
What you'll be doing... 
Lead and support defined workstreams within Project Orchestra, delivering high-impact commercial initiatives across Bauer Media Outdoor’s European markets. 
Support the development and execution of initiatives across commercial brand, product strategy, data strategy and organisational transformation. 
Provide structured, insight-driven analysis on commercial topics such as product packaging, pricing, yield management and inventory utilisation. 
Use data and commercial insight to support better decision-making, including analysis of sales performance, pricing outcomes and market trends. 
Translate complex data into clear, compelling, executive-ready narratives that drive action. 
Provide hands-on project support to local commercial teams, helping turn ideas into structured plans and executable actions. 
Ensure strategic initiatives are well-scoped, tracked and aligned to wider business objectives. 
Act as a problem-solver and thought partner to senior commercial leaders across markets. 
Work collaboratively across an international, matrix environment, building strong relationships with stakeholders at all levels. 
In the short term, the role will have a strong focus on supporting the successful integration of Bauer’s commercial functions through Project Orchestra, including supporting the commercial workstream through execution in 2026 and 2027.
Who are you?
We’re looking for someone who is highly organised, commercially curious and confident working with both data and people. You don’t need to have all the answers, but you do need strong analytical thinking, clear communication skills and a proactive, problem-solving mindset.
You will bring:
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to summarise complex ideas clearly. 
Clear, confident communication skills, both written and verbal, with experience presenting to senior stakeholders. 
Experience supporting or managing strategic or commercial projects in a fast-paced, complex environment. 
Strong relationship-building and influencing skills across diverse stakeholder groups. 
Confidence working independently to source and interpret data from a range of systems and teams. 
Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills. 
Comfort operating across an international, cross-market environment with different cultures and ways of working.
What's in it for you... 
Salary DOE + Annual Core Bonus 
Flexible working and a hybrid setup that gives the freedom to have your work -life balance. (This usually looks like 3 days in the office and 2 days working from home).
Enjoy 25 paid holidays, including those well need bank holidays!
Company Pension Scheme Contribution of up to 8%
Stay healthy with our Healthcare Cash Plan
Life Insurance and group income protection scheme
Cycle to Work Scheme + Enjoy travel/season ticket loans and exclusive discounts with hundreds of retailers
Make a difference with our charity volunteer days. It feels great to give back!
At Bauer Media Outdoor, we are committed to treating everyone with fairness and respect, regardless of their race, gender, age, ethnicity, background, sexual orientation, disability, or any other differences. We work hard to foster an inclusive environment, a place you can be yourself. We support reasonable adjustment requests from candidates and employees. If you're interested in joining us to shape the future of out-of-home media, we'd love to hear from you.
If you need this job description in another format or need any adjustments for your application/interview process, please let us know at careers-uk@bauermediaoutdoor.com
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves leading and supporting commercial workstreams within Project Orchestra to drive high-impact commercial initiatives across European markets, focusing on brand, product, data strategy, and organizational transformation. Responsibilities include providing structured, insight-driven analysis on commercial topics like pricing and yield management, and translating complex data into executive-ready narratives to support decision-making.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate must be highly organized, commercially curious, and confident working with both data and people, possessing strong analytical thinking and clear communication skills. Required experience includes supporting or managing strategic/commercial projects in complex environments, strong relationship-building skills, and proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint.

title: Database Engineer
description_text:
As Database Engineer, you’ll design and maintain the robust, scalable databases that power our platform. You’ll ensure real-time ingestion, 99.99% uptime, compliance, and seamless integration with all our services, helping NHS teams work faster and smarter. 
Key Responsibilities 
Database Design: Build scalable relational databases (e.g. Azure SQL), optimise performance, and enable high-volume data pipelines. 
Integration: Support full-stack platform integrations including data flows triggered by Power Automate connectors 
Performance & Reliability: Ensure uptime, backup, replication, and disaster recovery. 
Security & Compliance: Enforce role-based access, encryption, and medical device compliance. 
Collaboration: Work closely with engineers, analysts, clinicians, and stakeholders to align with product goals. 
Documentation: Maintain clear schema/process documentation and contribute to long-term scaling strategy. 
Must-have: 
5+ years hands-on experience with SQL (SQL Server familiarity a plus) 
Strong proficiency with complex stored procedures incl. writing, debugging, and refactoring 
Proven experience with query optimisation incl. indexing strategies, execution plan analysis, query rewrites 
Experience with database performance tuning incl. partitioning, caching strategies, statistics management 
Experience implementing robust ETL/ELT processes with proper validation, error handling and audit trails 
Understanding of CDC and incremental loading strategies 
Understanding of data retention strategies and key rotation policiesExperience with Azure SQL including backup configuration, failover strategies, and point-in-time recovery 
Strong communicator, proficient in spoken and written English, problem-solver, and collaborator. 
Comfortable working as the primary database specialist on a small team 
Nice-to-have: 
Familiarity with Azure Hyperscale database 
Healthcare data security or high-compliance background 
Familiarity with Azure Data Factory, SSIS, or equivalent tooling 
Experience with Azure Key Vault, Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) and Always Encrypted 
💰 Competitive salary 
🏥 Private healthcare for you & your family 
🕒 Flexible hours, hybrid model – 2 days in Central London Office location. 
🚀 High-impact work in real NHS practices 
🔍 Learning, growth & purpose-driven innovation
ai_benefits: Private healthcare for you & your family
ai_core_responsibilities: The Database Engineer will design and maintain scalable relational databases, ensuring real-time data ingestion and high uptime for the platform. Key duties include optimizing performance, supporting platform integrations, and managing backup and disaster recovery procedures.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess over 5 years of hands-on SQL experience, including strong proficiency in writing and debugging complex stored procedures and optimizing queries using indexing and execution plans. Experience with ETL/ELT processes, incremental loading strategies, and Azure SQL configuration is essential.

title: Project Manager (IT)
description_text:
Lead. Deliver. Innovate. Become Our Next Project Manager!
Are you a tech-savvy leader who thrives on turning bold ideas into reality? 
Rewardsco is looking for a Project Manager who can drive cutting-edge technology projects from concept to completion on time, on budget, and with impact!
Location: Sandton, Johannesburg
In This Role, You Will:
Lead the end-to-end delivery of technology projects, ensuring alignment with organisational strategy, governance, and delivery frameworks.
Partner with Product Owners, technical leads, and business stakeholders to translate strategic objectives into clear delivery plans, milestones, and outcomes.
Establish and maintain robust project governance, including risk, issue, dependency, change, and financial management, with clear reporting to senior leadership.
Act as the central coordination point across cross-functional teams and vendors, facilitating collaboration and managing inter-team and inter-project dependencies.
Ensure delivery outputs meet agreed quality, performance, security, and compliance standards, with structured handover into operations and support.
Manage project budgets, resource planning, vendor deliverables, and cost tracking in line with approved business cases.
Support delivery teams by removing blockers, enabling readiness for delivery, and coordinating release, deployment, and post-release stabilisation.
Drive continuous improvement through retrospectives, lessons learned, and contribution to PMO standards, tools, and best practices.
A tertiary qualification in Project Management, Information Technology, Business, Engineering, or a related field.
Project management certification is advantageous.
A Minimum of 5–7 years’ experience managing technology projects.
Proven experience delivering projects in Agile, hybrid, and waterfall environments.
Experience managing multi-disciplinary delivery teams.
Strong stakeholder and vendor management experience.
Clear Criminal record.
Why Rewardsco?
Join a forward-thinking organisation where your expertise shapes how technology drives business success. Collaborate with passionate teams, tackle exciting challenges, and grow your career in a supportive, high-performance environment.
Comprehensive Health and Wellness: Free access to Kaelo Health Medical Insurance, Financial Wellness Support, Mental Health Counselling and on-site Pharmacy with an in-house Nurse
Top-Tier Resources and Amenities: Work with world-class technology, enjoy three in-house restaurants and take advantage of discounted grocery staples
Ready to make technology happen? Apply today and lead the change!
Click this link to apply via our Careers Portal: https://www.rewardscocareers.com/search-jobs/
By applying for this role you are accepting Rewardsco’s Terms and Conditions. Click here to view them:

title: Planning & Reporting Lead
description_text:
About Us 
Bauer Media Outdoor UK is one of the leading Out of Home media owners, with more than 33,000 advertising sites in our portfolio. Our talented team of over 650 people work in 14 locations across the country, creating and delivering some of the most memorable advertising campaigns in the UK. 
At Bauer Media Outdoor, we are committed to Creating the Future of Media. We’re at the forefront of the change in our industry, making innovative use of technology and realising the potential of Out of Home media. 
About the role 
We are looking for an experienced Planning and Reporting Lead who can commit to a fixed term contract of 18 months, to cover maternity leave. 
The Planning & Reporting Lead is responsible for ensuring that all Construction projects are effectively planned, tracked and reported, providing one joined-up and accurate view of delivery performance across the business. 
The role acts as the central coordination point between Delivery and wider business functions, ensuring that departments are aligned on priorities, capacity, timelines and risks, and that planning is embedded as a core discipline across all projects. 
Through strong governance, structured reporting and proactive stakeholder engagement, the role supports timely decision-making and ensures that delivery risks and dependencies are clearly visible and managed. 
This role is focused on operational coordination, planning rigour and performance reporting. 
Key Objectives 
Accuracy and reliability of construction plans and forecasts. 
On-time milestone achievement across projects. 
Early identification and escalation of delivery risks. 
Quality and consistency of management reporting. 
Stakeholder confidence in planning and coordination processes. 
What we’re looking for… 
Strong experience in operational planning, scheduling or performance reporting roles within delivery focused environments. 
Excellent coordination and stakeholder management skills across multiple departments. 
Highly organised, with strong attention to detail and follow-through. 
Confident working with data, dashboards and workflow tools (e.g. monday.com, PowerPoint, Power BI, Excel or similar). 
Comfortable operating in fast-paced delivery or project-based environments. 
Experience in construction, infrastructure, logistics or asset-based businesses is desirable. 
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Operations, Project Management or a related field, or equivalent practical experience 
Confident user of project planning, workflow and reporting tools 
Experience producing management information, dashboards and performance reports for senior stakeholders. 
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build effective working relationships across functions. 
What is in it for you 
Salary £55,000 of per annum 
Travel expenses covered 
Flexible working (3 days in the office Tuesday to Thursday, working from home Monday and Friday) and a hybrid setup that gives the freedom to have your work -life balance 
Enjoy 33 paid holidays, including those well need bank holidays! 
Company Pension Scheme Contribution of up to 8% 
Stay healthy with our Healthcare Cash Plan 
Life Insurance and group income protection scheme 
Cycle to Work Scheme + Enjoy travel/season ticket loans and exclusive discounts with hundreds of retailers 
Make a difference with our charity volunteer days. It feels great to give back! 
At Bauer Media Outdoor, we are committed to treating everyone with fairness and respect, regardless of their race, gender, age, ethnicity, background, sexual orientation, disability, or any other differences. We work hard to foster an inclusive environment, a place you can be yourself. We support reasonable adjustment requests from candidates and employees. If you're interested in joining us to shape the future of out-of-home media, we'd love to hear from you. 
If you need this job description in another format or need any adjustments for your application/interview process, please let us know at careers-uk@bauermediaoutdoor.com

A bachelor's degree in a related field or equivalent practical experience is preferred, along with excellent coordination and stakeholder management skills.

title: Internal Sales - Renewable Energy - Livingston, Scotland
description_text:
With the UK government’s ambitious goal of delivering 60GW of solar power by 2030, Solar Careers UK are on a mission to help recruit, train, and place over 43,000 new professionals into rewarding careers within the solar and battery storage industry.
Solar Careers UK is a division of Solar Energy UK, the trade association representing over 425 solar businesses across the country. Many of these companies specialise in delivering high-quality solar installations for domestic, commercial, and utility-scale projects.
For the industry's most in-demand jobs, we have been asked to assist with the recruitment of full-time teams from across all regions of the United Kingdom.
By completing this application Solar Careers UK can help connect you to Internal Sales live job vacancies that match your skills and experience, and provide guidance on the qualifications and training needed to pursue your chosen career in the solar sector.
New entrants welcome.
If you're looking to transition into a Internal Sales role from another sector, many transferable skills can make you a strong candidate and these are listed below.
HDM Solar are a rapidly growing wholesale company in the renewable energy sector and we require an Internal Sales person to continue to support the growth of our sales. 

As an internal sales person you will be a key aspect of the sales team within the Livingstone, Scotland branch, you will be responsible for fostering good relationships with our customers having a pleasant and amenable telephone manner. Having already gained a good knowledge of the renewable industry you will acquire good product knowledge and support our customers with any inquiries relating to products, orders, deliveries and any ad hoc queries. You will be targeted to gain new customers, understanding the customers needs and making effective sales pitches as well as maintaining and gaining further business from current customers. 
Key Responsibilities:
Sourcing new sales opportunities through inbound lead follow-up and outbound cold calls and emails
Routing qualified opportunities to the appropriate Business Development Managers for further development and closure
Process sales orders
Following up on leads 
Communicating with customers
Understanding customers’ needs and identifying sales opportunities
Preparing quotes
Updating the CRM system 
Work closely other departments to ensure accurate orders are sent out for delivery
Solar design systems (training provided)
Customer service
Requirements and Skills 
Proven internal sales experience
Track record of achieving targets
Strong phone presence and experience cold calling
Experience with online ordering an CRM systems
Excellent verbal and written communications skills
Strong listening and presentation skills
Ability to multi-task, prioritize, and manage time effectively
Proficiency in Microsoft office
Exceptional customer service skills
A proactive “can do” attitude 
Team player
What we offer:
- Competitive salary, depending on experience
- Company pension scheme
- 23 days holiday + bank holidays and extra leave for xmas
- Free on-site parking
Job Type: Full-time, permanent after successful completion of 6-month probationary period.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Internal Sales person will be responsible for sourcing new sales opportunities, processing sales orders, and communicating with customers. They will also prepare quotes and update the CRM system while working closely with other departments.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have proven internal sales experience and a strong phone presence, along with excellent communication skills. A proactive attitude and the ability to manage time effectively are also essential.

title: Travel Consultant - French speaking
description_text:
To strengthen our team in the UK, we are looking for a French-speaking Luxury Travel Consultant.
Immerse yourself in the world of luxury travel: As a Travel Consultant at Ten Lifestyle Group, an internationally leading lifestyle management company, you will serve a sophisticated premium clientele and design tailor-made journeys that meet the highest standards.
In this role, you will combine your expertise in travel with a keen sense for luxury, trends, and service quality. You are not just a consultant – you become a trusted point of contact for clients seeking exceptional experiences worldwide.
Responsibility:
Advising our clients – premium cardholders – by phone and email in both French and English 
Handling customer inquiries from quote preparation to billing for individual trips 
Making reservations with airlines, hotels, and travel agencies 
Creating tailored offers, travel documents, and detailed travel itineraries 
Passionately advising and facilitating unique travel experiences 
Actively selling special promotions and exclusive offers 
Your Profile: 
You have excellent language skills in professional French and English, in written and spoken (C2 according to The Common European Framework) 
Ability to speak an additional language such as German or Italian is an advantage 
Have completed tertiary qualification or equivalent training, ideally in the field of tourism 
Have several years of professional experience in the travel industry, a tour operator or in a travel agency 
Have GDS knowledge (preferably Amadeus) 
Have excellent destination knowledge across key global regions 
Have a strong passion for everything related to travel and the luxury lifestyle, including gastronomy, events and culture 
Are service-oriented, flexible and able to work under pressure 
Have a positive attitude and identify with Ten Group values 
Have strong communication skills 
Value exceptional service and live by this motto 
Are available on a full-time basis (40hrs per week) 
Flexibility: Willingness to work 5 days a week between 08:00 and 20:00 GMT, Sunday to Thursday 
Guidelines for Hybrid/Home Office option:
Located in United Kingdom
Please note that if you live within a commutable distance of the office you will be asked to enter into a hybrid working arrangement - at least 2x a week in the office
A secure home office at your confirmed address, free from background noise or other distractions.
You must meet our minimum internet speeds if you want to work remotely / in our hybrid model and this will be checked during the recruitment process and again when you join. We also have a great office that you can work from as an alternative. To check your internet speed please click here, follow the instructions and once you have your results, please take a screenshot and upload this on your application form.
Our people are at the heart of our business, and we have a culture of recognition and reward—both through regular appraisals and through our annual Extra Mile Awards, where we celebrate those who have gone above and beyond in their role. We also encourage all staff to incorporate their aspirations and interests into their career at Ten, and we are there every step of the way to support their development.

title: SHEQ Manager
description_text:
Deep Manufacturing Inc. is seeking a SHEQ Manager to lead our Safety, Health, Environment, and Quality programs across our Houston manufacturing operations. This role ensures compliance with ISO 9001, ISO 45001, ISO 14001, and U.S. regulatory and industry standards including OSHA, EPA, API, ASME, and DNV.
You will drive safety culture, oversee SHEQ systems, and lead a small specialist team supporting both domestic and international projects.
Key Responsibilities
SHEQ Leadership
Develop, manage, and maintain the Integrated Management System (IMS).
Ensure compliance with OSHA, EPA, and applicable federal/state regulations.
Lead SHEQ governance meetings and report KPIs to senior leadership.
Effective review and acceptance of critical supplies to ensure conformance with contract specifications; including welding consumables, steel stock and associated certificates.
Alignment with UK SHEQ
Health & Safety
Oversee risk assessments (JHA, HAZCOM, industrial hygiene).
Ensure compliance with OSHA welding, machine guarding, electrical safety, fall protection, and lifting standards.
Manage LOTO procedures, hot‑work permitting, and emergency response programs.
Promote a strong safety culture through audits, toolbox talks, and near‑miss reporting.
Quality Management
Maintain and improve the Quality Management System (QMS) to ensure ISO 9001 compliance.
Support DNV requirements for WAAM operations and documentation.
Apply API and ASME standards to welding, fabrication, and pressure‑related work.
Lead internal, customer, and third‑party audits.
Drive root‑cause analysis and corrective action processes (8D, 5‑Why, FMEA).
Environmental Management
Maintain ISO 14001 compliance.
Oversee waste management, emissions, hazardous materials handling, and EPA‑regulated programs.
Support sustainability initiatives.
Leadership & Engagement
Champion a strong SHEQ culture across the organisation.
Engage leaders and teams to build shared ownership and accountability for Quality and Health & Safety outcomes.
Coach and support the business to embed SHEQ principles into everyday work, not just systems and processes.
Drive continuous improvement through ongoing alignment and collaboration with the UK SHEQ team, ensuring consistency, learning, and evolution of SHEQ practices across regions.
Act as a visible role model and trusted advisor, promoting continuous improvement and positive SHEQ behaviours.
Perform additional duties as assigned by the General Manager.
Key Deliverables
Maintain ISO 9001, ISO 45001, and ISO 14001 certifications.
Achieve zero major non‑conformances in audits.
Maintain DNV compliance for WAAM processes.
Deliver accurate monthly SHEQ performance reports.
Support SHEQ alignment across global operations.
.Essential
5+ years of SHEQ leadership experience in manufacturing or welding.
Auditor certification (ISO 9001/14001/45001).
Strong knowledge of ISO 9001, ISO 45001, ISO 14001.
Working knowledge of OSHA, EPA, and U.S. regulatory compliance.
Experience with API, ASME, DNV, and maritime/energy/O&G sector standards.
Internal and external audit experience.
Knowledge of WAAM or ISO 3834 welding quality standards.
Desirable
CSP, CIH, CHMM, or NEBOSH Diploma.
Familiarity with ASTM specifications for materials and testing. 
Good technical understanding of engineering drawings and fabrication methods.
Knowledge of NDE methods 
Skills
Strong leadership and communication abilities.
Analytical, detail‑oriented, and strong problem‑solving capability.
Ability to influence effectively at all organizational levels.
Working Conditions
On‑site role with regular presence on the production floor.
PPE required in all designated production areas.
Occasional travel to customer, supplier, or certification‑body sites.
Occasional planned international travel to support global expansion.
What's in it for you
Pay - Competitive
Bonus – Performance related bonus scheme
Benefits: Medical Insurance, 401k, Paid Time Off
About DEEP Manufacturing
We are a pioneering startup specialising in Wire Arc Additive Manufacturing (WAAM) technology. We produce low-volume, large-scale, high integrity metal components for safety critical applications across defence, aerospace, marine, offshore and energy sectors. Our mission is to transform traditional manufacturing through advanced additive processes, ensuring quality, innovation and sustainability.
Based at our offices in Harris County, Houston Tx, DEEP Manufacturing's capability enables the production of complex, large format components with significantly reduced lead times and material waste compared to traditional forging and casting.
Scale. Quality. Speed.
These principles guide how we design, manufacture and deliver. We combine industrial-scale capability with uncompromising quality standards and raid delivery to transform how large metal components are made.
About DEEP
DEEP is an international design and engineering company on a mission to make humans aquatic. W'er doing this by advancing ocean exploration through the development of new technologies that will enable humans to live and work in underwater environments for longer periods of time.
95% of the ocean is unexplored. Countless discoveries are waiting to be made and knowledge gained that will benefit our planet and all of humanity. The challenge is access and that's where DEEP comes in. We're building subsea human habitats to enable research at depth and increase our understanding of the ocean so we can help protect it.
DEEP Manufacturing was created to support DEEP's mission: using WAAM technology to overcome the long lead times associated with traditional casting and forging.
Working at DEEP
We're a team of highly experienced engineering, operations and safety experts, with a funding model that allows us the freedom to think long term and pursue our mission without traditional restraints. We want to drive progress at the forefront of deep-sea innovation and discovery to become stewards of the ocean and reshape our relationship with our planet.
As an organization with multi-generational objectives and ambitious goals, the values we bring to work every day are critical:
Be Curious – Find a better way and ask why not?". Answers are easy, questions are hard – ask original questions and be a curiosity catalyst!
Be Exceptional – It will take the most intelligent, talented and creative people on the planet working together to achieve DEEP's mission. Get it done, be relentless in your pursuit of excellence, and demand excellence from everyone.
Be Fearless – No one has done this on the scale we're doing it before. Impossible things must be done for the first time. We are the team pushing boundaries!
Be Good – Cultivate passion and thrive together. Be safe, take intelligent risks, but never compromise the safety of the team – ever!
At DEEP, we celebrate and embrace diversity and inclusion and strongly believe that the best innovations come from diverse perspectives. We invite applicants from all backgrounds to join us in creating technology that makes a meaningful impact on humanity, our oceans and planet.
ai_core_responsibilities: Lead and manage Safety, Health, Environment, and Quality programs across manufacturing operations. Ensure compliance with various ISO standards and U.S. regulations while promoting a strong safety culture.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 5 years of SHEQ leadership experience in manufacturing or welding, along with auditor certification for ISO standards. Strong knowledge of regulatory compliance and experience with industry standards is essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Scale. Quality. Speed.
linkedin_org_locations: Unit 4, Portside Park, Kings Weston Lane, Avonmouth, Bristol, BS11 8AR, GB
linkedin_org_description:
DEEP Manufacturing is an industrial additive manufacturing company specialising in the rapid production of large-scale, low-volume metal components for multiple industries including energy, subsea, aerospace and defence sectors.

Using DED-Arc technology, we provide an alternative to traditional forging and casting, offering fully-additive and hybrid manufacturing solutions. This approach reduces lead times, material waste and supply-chain risk for safety-critical and high-value applications. Our work supports complex infrastructure programmes delivering scale, quality and speed.

Originally established to manufacture subsea pressure vessels for human occupancy, DEEP Manufacturing can now support commercial customers across global energy, maritime and aerospace markets. We operate one of the largest concentrations of DED-Arc systems in Europe, based at our Advanced Manufacturing Centre of Excellence in the UK. This is supported by an expanding international footprint, including a manufacturing facility in the United States and active engagement with customers and industry stakeholders across the Middle East and Asia.

Our advanced multi-arm DED-Arc capability enables the manufacture of components up to 6.2 metres (20.3ft) in diameter and 3.2 metres (10.5ft) in height. This supports applications such as pressure vessels, structural components and large-format assemblies. Independent certification, including DNV AoM (Approval of Manufacture) covering pressure vessels, pressure vessels for human occupancy, boilers, hull structures and equipment, which underpins our ability to deliver for safety-critical environments.

DEEP Manufacturing works with end users, OEMs and engineering companies seeking scalable, certified metal manufacturing solutions for energy, subsea, aerospace and defence programmes worldwide.

title: Travel Consultant - French speaking
description_text:
To strengthen our team in the UK, we are looking for a French-speaking Luxury Travel Consultant.
Immerse yourself in the world of luxury travel: As a Travel Consultant at Ten Lifestyle Group, an internationally leading lifestyle management company, you will serve a sophisticated premium clientele and design tailor-made journeys that meet the highest standards.
In this role, you will combine your expertise in travel with a keen sense for luxury, trends, and service quality. You are not just a consultant – you become a trusted point of contact for clients seeking exceptional experiences worldwide.
Responsibility:
Advising our clients – premium cardholders – by phone and email in both French and English 
Handling customer inquiries from quote preparation to billing for individual trips 
Making reservations with airlines, hotels, and travel agencies 
Creating tailored offers, travel documents, and detailed travel itineraries 
Passionately advising and facilitating unique travel experiences 
Actively selling special promotions and exclusive offers 
Your Profile: 
You have excellent language skills in professional French and English, in written and spoken (C2 according to The Common European Framework) 
Ability to speak an additional language such as German or Italian is an advantage 
Have completed tertiary qualification or equivalent training, ideally in the field of tourism 
Have several years of professional experience in the travel industry, a tour operator or in a travel agency 
Have GDS knowledge (preferably Amadeus) 
Have excellent destination knowledge across key global regions 
Have a strong passion for everything related to travel and the luxury lifestyle, including gastronomy, events and culture 
Are service-oriented, flexible and able to work under pressure 
Have a positive attitude and identify with Ten Group values 
Have strong communication skills 
Value exceptional service and live by this motto 
Are available on a full-time basis (40hrs per week) 
Flexibility: Willingness to work 5 days a week between 08:00 and 20:00 GMT, Sunday to Thursday 
Guidelines for Hybrid/Home Office option:
Located in United Kingdom
Please note that if you live within a commutable distance of the office you will be asked to enter into a hybrid working arrangement - at least 2x a week in the office
A secure home office at your confirmed address, free from background noise or other distractions.

Ten partners with global financial institutions and other premium brands to attract and retain wealth and mass affluent clients. 

Millions of members have access to Ten's services across travel, dining, entertainment, lifestyle and retail benefits on behalf of over fifty clients. Our market-leading platform and service delivers a seamless experience and unparalleled access for your most important clients.

Ten’s leading technology-enabled travel and lifestyle platform provides unmatched concierge services, bespoke loyalty programmes, tailored benefits and experiences, and personalised and targeted communications.

Established in the UK in 1998, and a certified B Corp™ business, Ten operates in over 20 cities worldwide. Our service is delivered in 26 languages including English, French, German, Portuguese, Spanish, Mandarin, Cantonese and Japanese.  The service is available 24/7, 365 days a year.

Ten is owned by Ten Lifestyle Group Plc, a publicly listed company on the London Stock Exchange ('TENG').

title: Travel Consultant - French speaking
locations_alt_raw: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
locations_derived: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
description_text:
To strengthen our team in South Africa, we are looking for a French-speaking Luxury Travel Consultant. 
Immerse yourself in the world of luxury travel: As a Travel Consultant at Ten Lifestyle Group, an internationally leading lifestyle management company, you will serve a sophisticated premium clientele and design tailor-made journeys that meet the highest standards.
In this role, you will combine your expertise in travel with a keen sense for luxury, trends, and service quality. You are not just a consultant – you become a trusted point of contact for clients seeking exceptional experiences worldwide.
Responsibilities:
Advising our clients – premium cardholders – by phone and email in both French and English 
Handling customer inquiries from quote preparation to billing for individual trips 
Making reservations with airlines, hotels, and travel agencies 
Creating tailored offers, travel documents, and detailed travel itineraries 
Passionately advising and facilitating unique travel experiences 
Actively selling special promotions and exclusive offers 
Your Profile:
You have excellent language skills in professional French and English, in written and spoken (C2 according to The Common European Framework) 
Ability to speak an additional language such as German or Italian is an advantage 
Have completed tertiary qualification or equivalent training, ideally in the field of tourism 
Have several years of professional experience in the travel industry, a tour operator or in a travel agency 
Have GDS knowledge (preferably Amadeus) 
Have excellent destination knowledge across key global regions 
Have a strong passion for everything related to travel and the luxury lifestyle, including gastronomy, events and culture 
Are service-oriented, flexible and able to work under pressure 
Have a positive attitude and identify with Ten Group values 
Have strong communication skills 
Value exceptional service and live by this motto 
Are available on a full-time basis (40hrs per week) 
Flexibility: Willingness to work 5 days a week between 07:00 and 22:00 SAST, Sunday to Thursday 
Guidelines for Hybrid/Home Office option:
Located in South Africa
Please note that if you live within a commutable distance of the office you will be asked to enter into a hybrid working arrangement - at least 2x a week in the office
A secure home office at your confirmed address, free from background noise or other distractions.
You must meet our minimum internet speeds if you want to work remotely / in our hybrid model and this will be checked during the recruitment process and again when you join. We also have a great office that you can work from as an alternative. To check your internet speed please click here, follow the instructions and once you have your results, please take a screenshot and upload this on your application form.
Our people are at the heart of our business, and we foster a culture of recognition and reward—both through regular appraisals and our annual Extra Mile Awards, where we celebrate those who go above and beyond in their roles. We also encourage all staff to incorporate their aspirations and interests into their careers at Ten, and we support their development every step of the way.
Rewards Designed Around You
Competitive Salary based on experience, plus a performance-based bonus. Additional Late Shift and Sunday Shift allowances increase earning potential.
Hybrid Working: Combine working from home with working from the office. If you don’t live near our office, we also offer fully remote working options.
Paid Time Away from Work: Enjoy a competitive paid time-off package, including one day each year to volunteer for a cause that matters to you.
Paid Sabbaticals: One month of paid sabbatical after every five years of service, without using annual leave.
Extra Rewards: Access our lucrative Ten Loyalty Rewards program.
Remote Working Holidays: Opportunities to travel and work!
Employee Discounts: Enjoy great travel and entertainment discounts—just like our clients’ members do!
Medical Aid Contribution: Company contribution toward medical aid.
Late Shift Transport: Transport home for employees working late shifts (applies to those without a car).
ICAS Employee Health and Wellness (EHWP): Confidential and free services for all employees.
Be part of our global, dynamic, and inclusive team, with diversity at its core. 
Explore genuine career opportunities within a dynamic and international company.
Who We Are
Ten Lifestyle Group is a global luxury concierge service, and our travel department offers a unique opportunity to join a vibrant team. Our clients and colleagues are the cornerstones of what we do, and we serve High-Net-Worth (HNW) and Ultra-High-Net-Worth (UHNW) clients with their leisure travel requests.
At Ten, our goal is simple: to become the most trusted service business in the world.
We deliver our service through a combination of Ten’s proprietary, technology-enabled platform and the expertise of our highly trained lifestyle managers. Ten is growing quickly and has ambitious plans to keep innovating, inspiring, and improving the lives of millions of members.
As a Certified B Corp, Ten Lifestyle Group is part of a global community of businesses united by a shared goal: making a positive impact on society and the environment.
To find out more about Ten, please watch this short video Welcome to Ten
Commitment to Diversity
We encourage diverse philosophies, cultures, and experiences. We appreciate diversity and are dedicated to creating an inclusive work environment for our employees. This idea unites the teams at TEN. All aspects of our relationship, including the decision to hire, promote, discipline, or terminate, will be based on merit, competence, performance and business needs.
“Ten works with a small preferred supplier list of recruitment agencies only. Please note we are not accepting any further recruitment agencies at this time. Ten Group is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.”
Thank you for considering a career with us. Don't miss the opportunity to join our team!

title: Solution Architect - Core Banking (m/f/d)
description_text:
As Solution Architect – Core Banking (m/f/d), you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of modern banking architectures. You will drive strategic transformation projects, design scalable and future-proof solutions, and advise our clients on the modernization and optimization of their core banking platforms. 
Leveraging your deep understanding of core banking and related technology, you will collaborate closely with our customers to define and deliver solutions that enable operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and business agility. 
With your in-depth process and solution expertise, you are recognized as an expert in your field. You will work hand in hand with stakeholders across business and IT to translate strategic goals into concrete architectures and deliver tangible value. 
Key Responsibilities:
Engage with prospects, customers, and sales teams to comprehensively understand and anticipate business requirements and transformation challenges in core banking. 
Utilize your deep industry and technology knowledge to clearly articulate customer needs and solution approaches. 
Translate complex banking processes into robust, scalable solution designs and architectures based on SAP Fioneer products 
Lead the design of suitable target architectures and scalable solutions for complex business problems 
Conduct fit-gap analyses, identify innovation potential, and ensure solution designs align with customers’ strategic and business objectives, also considering regulatory guardrails. 
Advise customers on the best possible use of SAP Fioneer solutions to achieve business outcomes, e.g., modernization, cost efficiency, and long-term competitiveness. 
Drive opportunities, transformation projects, and innovation initiatives - acting as a trusted advisor and architectural lead for core banking architecture and solutions. 
Collaborate with product and engineering teams to shape product roadmaps and ensure alignment with market needs. 
Provide coaching to team members and foster a culture of knowledge sharing 
Academic degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, IT, Business, Finance, Economics, Physics, Mathematics; MBA or PhD is a plus. 
5+ years of professional experience in consulting, banking, or software companies – ideally in solution / enterprise architecture, technology transformation, and/or core banking implementation based on SAP Banking components. 
Proven track record in delivering complex transformation or modernization programs within commercial and/or retail banking. 
Deep expertise in core banking domains, such as deposits, lending, account management, product engines, and regulatory processes. 
Strong understanding of modern banking technology stacks, integration architectures, and cloud transformation. 
Excellent communication skills in English, German is a plus. 
We offer a comprehensive benefits package that includes occupational pension provisions, support for health and wellbeing, various mobility options like bike leasing and transportation allowances, as well as additional perks such as celebration rewards, meal programs, jubilee recognition, and relocation reimbursement.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Solution Architect will engage with clients to understand their core banking transformation needs and design scalable solutions. They will lead projects, conduct fit-gap analyses, and advise on the use of SAP Fioneer solutions.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have an academic degree in relevant fields and over 5 years of experience in consulting or banking, particularly in solution architecture. Expertise in core banking domains and modern banking technology stacks is essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Building software solutions for banks and insurance companies based on rock-solid technology and bold creativity.
linkedin_org_locations: 51 Eastcheap, City of London, England EC3M 1JA, GB
linkedin_org_description:
SAP Fioneer builds software and platforms that enable banks, insurance companies, and challengers to run, transform and grow – cost-efficiently and at scale and speed.   

Backed by global technology leader SAP and entrepreneurial investor Dediq, we are in a unique position to combine the speed and agility of a start-up with the experience and capabilities of a best-in-class software company. Benefiting from a broad network of partners, we aspire to be more than just a vendor: a reliable and safe pair of hands that is there today and tomorrow to put financial services organizations at the cutting edge – from established players to disruptive challengers.  

As a global business with over 1,500 employees in 22 offices, and over 1,200 customers across 17 countries, SAP Fioneer’s pioneering spirit is fueled by bold creativity and underpinned by its rock-solid technology.

title: Χειριστές Μηχανημάτων
description_text:
Η εταιρεία ΟΔΟΣ ΑΤΕ, μία από τις παλαιότερες και ισχυρότερες εταιρείες κατασκευής έργων Υποδομής και μεγάλη εξειδίκευση στα οδοστρώματα, αναζητά έμπειρους χειριστές μηχανημάτων.
Έναρξη συνεργασίας: Άμεσα
Περιοχές έργων: Αττική, Αιτωλοακαρνανία, Ηλεία,θεσσαλια,Βοιωτια
Χειρισμός βαρέων μηχανημάτων σε χωματουργικές και οδοποιητικές εργασίες
Συμμόρφωση με οδηγίες μηχανικού και εργοδηγού
Τήρηση κανόνων Υγείας & Ασφάλειας
Εκσκαφέα ελαστιχοφόρου και ερπυστριοφόρου με σφυρί
Μπουλντόζας – προώθησης και διάστρωσης γαιών
Διαμορφωτήρα - grader
Oδοστρωτήρα δονητικού
Φορτωτή ελαστιχοφόρου
Φορτωτή ερπυστριοφόρου
Εκσαφεα Φορτωτη Τυπου (Backhoe Loader) JCB 
Γερανοφορου Οχηματος (Παπαγαλος)
Η Εταιρεία προσφέρει ιδιαίτερα ανταγωνιστικό πακέτο αποδοχών, καλό εργασιακό περιβάλλον, εργασία και σύγχρονα – καινούρια μηχανήματα, σταθερός μισθός, εγγυημένες αποδοχές, αορίστου χρόνου σύμβαση, δυνατότητα εξέλιξης.
Γίνετε μέρος των ομάδων εργασίας μας, σ' ένα περιβάλλον ανάπτυξης, στα πλαίσια μιας ευημερούς συνεργασίας με την εταιρία ΟΔΟΣ ΟΤΕ.
Συμμετέχετε στην δημιουργία έργων ποιότητας.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves operating heavy machinery for earthworks and road construction projects. Operators must comply with instructions from engineers and site managers while adhering to health and safety regulations.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience in operating various types of heavy machinery, including excavators and bulldozers. Compliance with safety standards and teamwork is essential.

title: Junior/Mid Performance Marketing Manager - International Client Lead - D2C Team
description_text:
At Front Row, we partner with leading brands to accelerate their ecommerce growth. We leverage our capabilities and proprietary technology to design, market, distribute and accelerate brands on a global scale. We’re continually cultivating functional areas of expertise and retaining the highest caliber of talent — while sharing knowledge and data, creating efficiencies, and looking at every aspect of our client’s business from a 360° perspective. We work effectively to give every client the hands-on support, niche knowledge, and first-access they need to win in the world’s most competitive commerce markets and make each of our six capabilities the first to act on the next big things. 
We live for the exchange of energy between brands and audiences, you should too, and we’ve made it our mission to expand every brand’s market share and heart share. Because we believe every transaction has the potential to be transformational. 
About our Bratislava Digital Marketing Team
We're the team that used to be BizMut, now merged under Front Row.
We’re numbers-driven performance marketers who focus on our clients’ pipeline revenue and bottom-line value. We specialize in building and executing performance marketing strategies for B2B, D2C and Amazon clients. 
As we grow the D2C team in Bratislava, we are looking for innovative and fresh perspectives. 
Are you a hands-on digital marketer with experience in paid direct-to-consumer e-commerce marketing?
Are Google Ads, Google Merchant Center and Google Analytics your strong suite?
Come on board and join our growing D2C department!
How would you spend your time if you join us?
ABOUT 60% @ CAMPAIGN & PROJECT MANAGEMENT:
• Manage client’s projects and hold responsibility for delivery of quantifiable results
• Implement strategies, which you collaborated on creating, across all Google Ads products, i.e. Search Ads, Shopping Ads, PMax, YouTube, Display, Discovery, Merchant Center; and also Meta Ads (and other paid social) 
• Manage the media spend of large scale, international campaigns across these platforms with the goal of reaching specific, measurable business goals.
• Optimise marketing campaigns in relation to clients’ goals.

ABOUT 25% @ CLIENTS’ COMMS:
• Educate, communicate & manage relationships with senior-management from your client portfolio.
• Regularly update reports & improve reporting of results for your clients.
• Prepare reports in PowerPoint, video & other formats for clients, to help them understand where they are at & how to best move forward in their digital marketing tactics and strategy.

ABOUT 15% @ YOUR PERSONAL DEVELOPMENT & LEARNING:
• You will participate in growing our D2C team. 
• You will learn to create, test and calibrate digital marketing strategies for clients, based on their goals and needs.
•You will get the chance to create or collaborate on creation of internal wiki & external blog articles in your area of expertise.
• You will work closely alongside experienced professionals and have a lot of opportunities to develop skills that will serve you throughout your career
• You will have plenty of shadowing opportunities with TOP leaders in the field & managing directors of our company - Niall Donohoe and Bjoern Sjut
Who Are You?
You have excellent communications skills in English, both written and oral (minimum level C1).
You have a minimum of 2 year of proven hands-on experience in managing D2C E-Commerce marketing campaigns.
You have experience with Google Ads, Google Merchant Center and Google Analytics.
You are familiar in testing with Google's Ad products beyond Search Ads (i.e Shopping, PMax, YouTube, Display, Discovery).
You have experience with Meta Ads (Catalog Ads, ASC+ and other campaign types)
You have a down to earth personality – we don't take ourselves too seriously and like to have fun & joy at what we do.
You are super curious & have a drive to be good at what you do and constantly looking to improve and learn.
You are an analytical, contextual & critical thinker.
You are a problem solver who doesn’t get frustrated by solving number related puzzles – you enjoy figuring out why something doesn’t work and making it work.
You have great presentation skills & happy to be in a client-facing role as a data-driven storyteller.
You are proficient @Excel & can analyse numerical data & synthesize them into conclusions.
You are keen on sharing your knowledge both internally & externally.
Your attention to detail borders on the extreme and you are proud of that.
You are a master at considering priorities and independently deciding what to pay attention to first.
You thrive in an upbeat, deadline-driven and fast-growing environment and don’t mind working on multiple projects simultaneously.
Not a deal breaker but would be great if you also have:
Knowledge and experience in running paid campaigns on other platforms like Tiktok, Pinterest, Reddit 
Experience with Shopify
What's in it for you? 
Starting salary in the range of 1500-2500 EUR gross per month (* the final salary offered will be based on the candidate's experience)
This is a full-time role based in Bratislava. 
Extra vacation days
Flexible working hours
Flexible place of work - 3 home office days per week
Private pension contribution (3rd pillar)
Summer office – this year’s destination is Paris
100% pay on sick leave for the first 2 days of illness
100% coverage of meal contribution & Multisport Card
Mobile phone contract for both business and personal use
2 hours per week of fixed time for reading & learning of your choice
Unlimited education budget & hands-on support in your learning path
Employee Wellbeing Program – Work & Life Coaching & Counselling with our external partner
Regular team building & team spirit events (monthly Team Breakfasts, Games nights, Cultural Nights, Table Tennis tournament and much more)
Annual volunteer days
Employee Referral Program
Relaxed and dog-friendly work environment
International team with English as our main language
Hybrid work in our brand-new office in Bratislava Old Town
International relocation support can be agreed on if needed
Working on large-scale, international marketing campaigns for well-known brands in an international environment
Working with and learn from some of Germany’s top digital marketing minds
And much more...
What can you expect in terms of the process?

1. First Chat (30 minutes with our People & Culture team)
2. Practical Assignment (1.5 hours to test your skills)
3. Second Chat (~1.5 hours with your potential Manager & colleagues)
AI Usage Disclaimer:
We understand that AI tools can be helpful in various stages of the application process. While we do not specifically encourage their use, we are open to candidates utilizing AI as long as the work reflects your own knowledge and understanding. Our primary focus is on assessing your genuine expertise, ability to apply concepts, and how you approach real-world scenarios. The use of AI will not negatively impact your candidacy, provided these conditions are met.

title: Head of Growth & Partnerships (The Curve Africa by Kora)
description_text:
Company
The Curve Africa by Kora is a social enterprise building Africa’s next generation of technology talent.
Our mission is to bridge the gap between formal education and employable tech skills by providing accelerated, high-quality training pathways for African youth, particularly those with limited access to opportunity. We equip talent with the technical, professional, and problem-solving skills needed to thrive in the modern digital economy.
Our vision is to build a scalable, inclusive pipeline of African technology talent prepared to solve real-world problems and lead innovation across the continent.
We are now entering a growth phase and are seeking a strategic leader to expand our partnerships, footprint, and long-term impact.
We currently deliver a six-month, intensive, project-based training program in Software Engineering and Product Design, combining real-world technical training, workplace and soft skills development, portfolio and interview readiness and mentorship and industry exposure.
Our long-term ambition is to train 10,000 young Africans in Software Engineering for free within 10 years.
Our Model
The Curve operates as a grant and partnership-funded social enterprise. Corporate CSR partnerships, grants and foundations and institutional and ecosystem collaborations drive our sustainability model.
Our work sits at the intersection of education, technology, and economic mobility, and directly contributes to the following UN Sustainable Development Goals:
SDG 4 – Quality Education
SDG 5 – Gender Equality
SDG 8 – Decent Work & Economic Growth
SDG 9 – Industry, Innovation & Infrastructure
SDG 10 – Reduced Inequalities
We are focused on bringing technology access and opportunity to the bottom of the pyramid, ensuring that talent, not privilege, determines access to the digital economy.
About the Role
The Head of Growth & Partnerships will be responsible for expanding The Curve’s reach, influence, and sustainability. This role leads external strategy, including partnerships, funding relationships, ecosystem positioning, and geographic/program expansion.
This is a builder role for someone who can turn vision into networks, and networks into opportunity.
Here are a couple of things you'll be doing:
Strategic Partnerships & Alliances:
Develop and execute The Curve’s partnerships strategy across corporates, foundations & funders, universities & institutions and ecosystem organisations.
Secure long-term, value-aligned partnerships that support funding, mentorship pipelines, and program delivery.
Lead sponsor and partner relationship management at a senior level.
Growth & Expansion Strategy:
Identify and evaluate new growth opportunities, including new countries or regions, digital program expansion and institutional collaborations.
Design scalable partnership models for program delivery.
Support the structuring of The Curve’s next phase of growth.
Funding & Sustainability Support:
Support fundraising strategy through partner proposals, institutional relationship development and strategic funding conversations.
Translate The Curve’s impact into compelling narratives for partners and stakeholders.
Ecosystem Positioning & Visibility:
Represent The Curve at high-level events, forums, and strategic conversations
Position The Curve as a leading platform for African leadership access
Build relationships with key ecosystem influencers, founders, and leaders.
Internal Strategic Support:
Work closely with program and operations teams to ensure partnerships align with delivery capacity.
Provide strategic input into program direction based on partner and ecosystem insight.
Here's what we are looking for:
5–8 years’ experience in partnerships, business development, ecosystem building, strategy, or growth roles, ideally across startups, impact organisations, corporates, or foundations.
Strong understanding of partnership development, stakeholder engagement, and cross-sector collaboration.
Proven ability to build and manage senior relationships with corporates, institutions, funders, and ecosystem leaders.
Confident in leading external conversations, representing an organisation, and translating vision into mutually valuable partnerships.
Excellent writing and documentation skills (proposals, partnership decks, concept notes, reports, and strategic briefs).
Strong organisational and tracking skills; able to manage pipelines, partnership lifecycles, and follow-through across multiple stakeholders.
Experience working within high-growth, mission-driven, or ecosystem-led environments is highly desirable.
Exposure to fundraising, sponsorships, CSR partnerships, or institutional collaborations is an advantage.
Strong strategic thinking ability; can connect partnerships to long-term organisational growth and impact.
Comfortable working across geographies and cultures, with sensitivity to diverse stakeholders.
High level of integrity, professionalism, and credibility when representing the organisation externally.
We’re looking for someone who is:
A natural relationship builder with strong executive presence
Experienced in partnerships, ecosystem building, or business development
Comfortable in corporate, startup, and nonprofit environments
Able to think strategically while moving fast
Passionate about access, equity, and leadership development in Africa
Experience working with foundations, donors, or CSR teams
Background in impact, education, leadership development, or community platforms
Experience expanding programmes across regions.
Note: We recognise imposter syndrome is real - any candidate who does not perfectly fit every characteristic of this role is still strongly encouraged to apply.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Head of Growth & Partnerships will expand The Curve’s reach and sustainability through strategic partnerships and growth initiatives. This role involves managing relationships with corporates, foundations, and institutions to support funding and program delivery.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 5-8 years of experience in partnerships, business development, or strategy roles, with a strong understanding of stakeholder engagement. Excellent writing skills and the ability to manage senior relationships are essential.

title: Junior Software Engineer (Data Products)
description_text:
What we’re looking for
As a Junior Software Engineer (Data Products) at Yapily, you will help build and scale our modern, next-generation data platform. This is a junior level role perfect for someone with a strong Java/Python foundation who is eager to specialize in the world of Data Engineering.
You will support the team in creating high-performance data pipelines, billing infrastructure, and self-serve data APIs. This role offers a unique front-row seat to the GCP (Google Cloud Platform) stack, where you’ll learn to develop data systems that empower engineering teams to turn raw data into actionable value.
Responsibilities
As a Junior Software Engineer, Data Products, you will be responsible for:
Supporting Data Pipelines: Assisting in the design and maintenance of scalable data ingestion and processing systems using ETL/ELT methodologies.
API Development: Helping build and document APIs that deliver a seamless data experience for internal and external stakeholders.
Database Operations: Working with SQL and NoSQL databases, helping to optimize schemas and write efficient queries to support high-volume transactions.
Cloud Resource Management: Learning to deploy and maintain software utilizing GCP services such as Pub/Sub, BigQuery, Cloud Storage
Billing Infrastructure Support: Contributing to a reliable billing architecture within an event-driven environment.
Cross-functional Collaboration: Partnering with BI, infrastructure, and product teams to understand data requirements and deliver technical solutions.
Quality & Monitoring: Participating in testing, monitoring, and logging practices to ensure system resilience.
Continuous Learning: Engaging in code reviews and agile ceremonies to rapidly level up your technical skills and product knowledge. 
Essential Skills
Java Foundation: 1–2 years of experience (including internships or intensive projects) in Java development. 
Database Basics: Academic or professional exposure to SQL; you should be comfortable writing queries and understand basic schema design.
Version Control: Familiarity with Git and a basic understanding of CI/CD concepts.
Problem-Solving Mindset: A proactive approach to debugging and a genuine curiosity about how data flows through a system.
API Awareness: Basic understanding of REST APIs and how microservices communicate.
Educational Background: A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
Preferred Skills (Nice to Have)
Python/Shell: Basic knowledge for scripting or data automation.
Cloud Exposure: Any experience with GCP, AWS, or Azure (Cloud certifications are a plus!).
Containerization: Familiarity with Docker or the concept of containerized applications.
Data Tools: Exposure to any data processing tools like Airflow, Kafka, or Spark.
Infrastructure as Code: A desire to learn tools like Terraform.
Financial Tech Interest: An interest in Open Banking and the FinTech landscape.
🌟 Why You’ll Love Working With Us
Competitive Pay & Equity – We offer a great base salary plus equity, so you’ll own a part of what we’re building together.
Generous Time Off – Enjoy 25 days of holiday each year (plus bank holidays if you’re in the UK), and earn an extra day each year after your first, up to 5 more!
Hybrid Working – Life’s about balance. you can work from home up to 3 days a week, eligibility criteria applies.
Nomad Working – Feel like a change of scenery? Work from anywhere for up to 30 days each year.
Family First – We offer enhanced Maternity and Paternity leave because your family matters
Private Medical Insurance – You’ll get top-notch cover through BUPA, because your health is a priority.
Mental Health Support – Access personalised mental wellness support through our award-winning partner.
Future-Ready Perks – Including a solid company pension, life assurance, and income protection.
Learn & Grow – A £200 annual budget for learning and personal development. Invest in you!
Cycle to Work Scheme – Commute the healthy way with support from our cycle to work programme.
Refer a Friend – Bring someone great onboard and earn £1,000 with our referral scheme.
Team Vibes – Monthly socials, team lunches, and a budget to hang out and have fun (yes, pizza included 🍕).
Office Snacks & Doggies – Daily snacks to keep you going, and yes – we’re proudly a dog-friendly office 🐾.
Our Values 
We obsess about quality
Our customers have entrusted us with a critical function in a regulated industry…and we take that responsibility seriously. We always assume ownership and hold ourselves accountable.
We are curious
Our innovation is powered by our collective growth mindset. We’re lifelong learners who challenge assumptions, experiment, and iterate.
We act with integrity
We’re guided by our mission and earn and maintain trust by doing what’s right, even when it’s not easy.
We are do-ers
We reject indifference and agility is our strength. 
We’re motivated by challenges, and biassed towards action.
We problem-solve together
We’re diverse people in diverse places, and know the best solutions are born out of collaboration. We win, lose, and learn…together.

You will also collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and contribute to the overall data architecture.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a foundation in Java and basic knowledge of SQL, along with familiarity with version control systems like Git. A degree in Computer Science or a related field is preferred, along with a proactive problem-solving mindset.

title: Systems Administrator
description_text:
IT Infrastructure & Support Specialist
📍 Teesside
We’re looking for an experienced IT Infrastructure & Support Specialist to join our team at our Teesside facility. This hybrid role combines hands-on technical support with systems and infrastructure management, making it ideal for someone who enjoys variety, problem-solving, and working closely with end users and stakeholders.
You’ll play a key role in keeping our IT environment secure, resilient, and running smoothly while also supporting colleagues with day-to-day technical needs and larger project work.
What You’ll Be Doing
Providing advanced technical support via help desk tickets, phone, email, and in-person requests
Managing, configuring, and supporting end-user devices (desktops, laptops, printers, peripherals, and mobile devices)
Administering Active Directory (users, groups, permissions, devices)
Supporting and maintaining Microsoft-based environments, including M365, Azure, VMWare, and virtualization platforms
Installing, configuring, and maintaining network systems, hardware, and software
Managing IT asset inventory and lifecycle (hardware, software, peripherals)
Supporting workstation moves, system upgrades, migrations, and new implementations
Acting as an escalation point for complex IT issues
Working with vendors and suppliers to diagnose, troubleshoot, and repair IT assets
Producing and maintaining technical documentation, processes, and procedures
Delivering technical training and knowledge transfer to IT team members
Communicating complex technical information clearly to both technical and non-technical users
Participating in project planning and delivery across the business
Providing out-of-hours and weekend support when required
What We’re Looking For
Strong experience with Microsoft operating systems, O365/M365, Azure, and networking technologies
Solid knowledge of LAN/WAN, security protocols, performance tuning, and troubleshooting
Experience with virtualisation (VMWare, Hyper-V), Citrix, and telephony systems
Confident using Active Directory, password security, and asset management tools
Minimum 3 years’ experience in:
PC hardware/software support, deployment, and troubleshooting
Supporting enterprise IT environments (networks, telephony, security)
Excellent communication skills and the ability to build trust at all levels of the organisation
Highly organised, adaptable, and able to prioritise multiple tasks effectively
Comfortable working independently and as part of a collaborative team
Professional, reliable, and customer-focused approach
Bachelor’s degree in a computer-related field or equivalent experience (preferred)
MCSE or equivalent certification (desirable)
Why Join WASL?
WASL also offers comprehensive benefit packages to its eligible employees. This includes:
Competitive salaries, flexible working patterns
33 days annual leave (pro-rota for part time) including UK bank holidays, holiday buy & sell scheme
Health & wellbeing support, company pension, cycle to work scheme, electric car scheme, retail discounts, referral bonus
Hybrid working with exposure to both infrastructure and end-user support
Opportunity to work on meaningful projects and system improvements
A varied, hands-on role with real ownership and impact
Supportive team environment with opportunities to grow and develop
About Us
Willis Aviation Services Limited are part of Willis Lease Finance Corporation, a pioneer and provider of global aviation services for over 45 years. Our facility at Teesside International Airport provides base maintenance, return to service, aircraft disassembly, short and long term aircraft storage, and ground and cargo handling services.
At Willis Aviation Services Limited (WASL), we are committed to fair hiring practices and building a diverse, respectful, and inclusive workplace. We value every individual’s contribution and make employment decisions based on job-related criteria such as qualifications, experience, and the ability to perform the role.
These are exciting times for Willis Aviation Services Limited as we continue to grow. If you’re ready to make a difference in aviation, apply today.
To Recruitment Agencies
WASL does not accept unsolicited CVs or candidate submissions from agencies. All unsolicited profiles will be treated as the property of WASL, and no fees will be paid.
This applies to any CVs sent directly to employees or managers without prior agreement with our internal Talent Acquisition team.

These leasing activities are integrated with engine and aircraft trading, engine lease pools and asset management services through Willis Asset Management Limited, as well as various end-of-life solutions for engines and aviation materials provided through Willis Aeronautical Services, Inc.
