title: Roof Terrace Maintenance Technician
description_text:
Job description
Position: Roof Terrace Maintenance Technician
Location: London
Contract: Full Time 40 hours per week, Permanent
Salary: Basic salary £28,232k per annum
Reporting To: Team Leader
Urban Planters has been bringing homes and workplaces to life with plants since 1965.
We deliver our products and services using our national network of branches, spanning the length and breadth of the UK. This means we can offer the personalised service of a local business but with the standards and resources of a national organisation.
Quality, safety, sustainability and professionalism underpin all that we do. To this end, we are ISO 9001, 14001 and 45001 certified.
We now have a carbon negative business who’s teams install planting in, on and around the buildings of the companies we partner with. We are happiest while we are landscaping, installing indoor planting schemes or maintaining the plants or grounds of our clients.
The Role
Working as a Roof Terrace Maintenance Technician, you will work on site ensuring the best possible service is provided to our ever growing exterior customer base.
This opportunity is for someone looking to join a busy, fast paced business where you will be working with a friendly and welcoming team. This is an excellent opportunity to join a leading company in the Interior and Exterior planting sector where progression and development are encouraged.
Your main duties will include:
Managing irrigation and watering to plants as required
A small amount of pedestrian Grass cutting and strimming
Shrub pruning, plant care
Weed maintenance and pest control
Complete all digital records required through smart phone application
Liaising with customers
Autumn clearing
Spring preparation
Qualifications & Experience:
Full UK drivers licence
Garden maintenance experience is essential
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Able to work as part of a team
Plant knowledge would be advantageous
Qualification in Horticulture is desirable but is not essential
As part of your role, you will be required to;
Always act in a professional manner as a representative of the organisation.
Comply with all relevant health and safety requirements
Ensure that the clients are always completely satisfied
Initiate suggestions to improve the organisations performance
Complete timesheets and other paperwork and ensure all is handed in promptly
This Opportunity is for someone looking to join a busy, fast paced business where you will be working with a friendly and welcoming team. This is an excellent opportunity to join a leading company in the Interior and Exterior planting sector where progression and development are encouraged. 
What we offer in return:
A Competitive Salary,
Job Stability & Security,
Progression Opportunities,
Employee Assistance Program,
Refer A Friend Bonus Scheme,
Company Events,

title: People Development Manager
description_text:
About us 
We are a leading consultancy with a purpose to make an enduring impact on health and healthcare. We work with leaders and frontline teams to improve health, transform healthcare, drive adoption of innovation and create value through investment. 
Our consultancy serves the entire healthcare sector, from payors and providers of care, to life science companies, health tech and sector suppliers and health investors. We provide end-to-end services, from strategy through implementation, accelerated by data, digital and AI. 
We shape opinion through evidence-based thought leadership on key issues affecting health. With unmatched ability to access and use health data, our consultants are a driving force for delivering positive and meaningful change. 
Our strategic intent 
We are focused on building the leading consulting company dedicated to health. We serve the entire healthcare sector, including healthcare systems (providers, payors and regulators), life sciences (pharmaceuticals, biotech, devices and diagnostics), health technology, health investors, and the wider supplier landscape. 
We provide end-to-end services, from strategy through implementation, supporting organisations to improve population health and healthcare outcomes. Our work spans strategy and transformation, finance and performance improvement, and delivery accelerated by data, digital and AI. We help clients understand their ambitions, identify opportunities to create value, apply innovation in practice, and deliver sustainable, measurable change. 
Our consulting is accelerated by data. With an unmatched ability to access and use health data, we are recognised for our expertise in its safe and responsible application, improving health and healthcare delivery, supporting adoption of innovation, generating evidence, and informing decision-making. Our engineering and data science capabilities underpin our consulting and are also deployed directly with clients, often as part of multidisciplinary teams. 
We are building a community of expert consultants who want to operate at the leading edge of the profession and who share a passion for health. Through structured career development from Analyst to Partner, underpinned by apprenticeship, mentorship and formal training, we are cultivating the leaders of the future and supporting individuals to develop distinctive expertise that creates value for our clients. 
Our Mission 
Our mission is to be invaluable to our clients, supporting them to innovate and make lasting improvements and to build an exceptional company that attracts, develops, and retains a trusted and uniquely talented team. 
About the role 
This is a rare opportunity to shape the people development function of a fast-growing health consultancy with serious ambitions. Reporting to the Chief People Officer, you will champion our People Strategy and own our performance and development frameworks, with enormous scope to make a difference. 
We have developed our People Strategy to support delivery of CF's company strategy, ensuring we are an employer of choice within the sector and an 'irresistible organisation' for our employees. We are committed to delivering 10 days of training per year to all staff through our in-house development programme, supported by external inputs. Our programme draws on the extensive skills and experience of our leadership team and has been accredited as a route to become Chartered Management Consultants. Your role? Spearhead and innovate this offering as we scale. 
Our growth ambitions are significant. We have diversified over the past seven years, and the future will likely see us establish additional UK bases and expand internationally. These developments have major implications for how we work and the skills we need – and you will be central to navigating this evolution. 
Responsibilities 
You will be the driving force behind how our people grow, perform, and thrive: 
Own Performance & Development 
Lead our performance management and skills framework, ensuring it is innovative, understood, and embedded across the organisation, with all talent artefacts and adjacent processes aligned to support it 
Run our Assessment and Development Committee, managing feedback processes and facilitating career progression discussions 
Act as a trusted advisor on giving and receiving feedback, readiness to progress, and career planning 
Champion our Development Sponsor framework, equipping sponsors with resources, training and support 
Coach managers in effective 1-to-1 conversations using a coaching leadership style 
Manage frameworks for probationary, monthly, mid-year and end-of-year review discussions 
Lead our 360 feedback process and help create development plans 
Drive our OKR programme, delivering training on how OKRs empower teams and individuals 
Collaborate with Resourcing and Practice leadership to stay abreast of development needs 
Engage in succession planning to identify top talent with Partner potential 
Transform Learning & Development 
Position the organisation as an employer of choice through compelling learning offerings that other consulting firms cannot beat 
Identify skills development needs and serve as a design consultant for leadership development programmes 
Refresh our annual L&D offering (with learning journeys for each career path) in line with skills gaps and business priorities 
Work with CF leaders, staff and external providers to build out the learning programme, ensuring it meets current needs and priorities 
Develop learning aims, approaches, formats and materials to deliver high-quality learning 
Create a portfolio of online training that addresses core needs flexibly 
Leverage e-learning platforms, gamification, and microlearning to enhance learning experiences 
Evolve our health and wellbeing learning offer based on employee feedback 
Refine our onboarding programme so new hires feel successful from day one 
Introduce an LMS to support e-learning content and on-demand learning 
Own annual accreditation processes with ChMC, CIMA & RSS 
Manage our learning calendar, including attendance and feedback management 
Evaluate and report on learning programme effectiveness 
Support the CPO in designing the leadership development programme 
Champion the external development programme, including our Chartered Management Consultant Award pathway and relationship with MCA/CMI 
Be accountable for and enhance impact of the learning budget 
Build Career Frameworks 
Own development of career pathways, maintaining role overviews, expectations, and growth ladders 
Enhance and maintain our skills matrix as a tool for development planning, resourcing, people planning and succession planning 
Develop and manage an experienced hires programme as a critical talent source 
Manage our secondment programme to create exciting opportunities for industry experience 
Create innovative development opportunities through shadowing and volunteering 
We’re not necessarily looking for someone with CIPD qualifications or specific degrees. We want a collaborative, innovative and friendly people professional who loves rolling up their sleeves and jumping into the thick of the action. Someone who is tech savvy, results oriented and delivers work to the highest standards. 
5+ years in a people role with hands-on L&D and performance management experience 
Understanding of and experience using different L&D methodologies and a basic knowledge of Learning Science 
Familiarity with different learning technologies and methods 
Experience with LMS administration 
Proven ability to work with stakeholders outside of HR and influence senior stakeholders 
Self-starter mentality, action-oriented and able to thrive in an entrepreneurial, fast-paced environment 
Exceptional communication and relationship-building skills to influence across all levels 
A data-driven approach: you make decisions and recommendations based on data to achieve people and business outcomes 
Experience managing projects and understanding of project management principles 
Desirable: 
Professional services background highly desirable 
Health background (health systems, life sciences and/or health investing) an added bonus 
Flexible working 
Our default is to work in person with our clients, but we also support remote working. Team members can work from home one day per week as standard, and we offer an additional 44 remote working days per year. This allows you to work from home up to two days per week-subject to client needs- or use your allowance in blocks, depending on what works best for you. Office hours are flexible within our core hours of 10am–4pm.
We offer a competitive and flexible reward package designed to support you at work and beyond it. You will benefit from a generous holiday allowance that grows with your career (minimum of 25 days), a strong employer pension contribution, and the freedom to tailor benefits to suit your lifestyle, from wellbeing and fitness to financial protection. 
We are committed to supporting life’s important moments, with enhanced family leave, income and life protection, and access to practical benefits that make everyday life easier, such as interest-free loans and travel support. 
Your wellbeing matters to us. You will have access to a comprehensive wellbeing and employee assistance programme, preventative health benefits, and initiatives that support an active, balanced way of working. 
Above all, we invest in our people; offering flexibility, security, and benefits that grow with you, so you can do your best work while building a sustainable and rewarding career.
ai_core_responsibilities: You will lead the performance management and skills framework, ensuring it is innovative and embedded across the organization. Additionally, you will drive the learning and development initiatives to position the organization as an employer of choice.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate should have over 5 years of experience in a people role with hands-on learning and development experience. Familiarity with different learning technologies and a self-starter mentality are also essential.

linkedin_org_locations: 1 Lyric Square, London, England W6 0NB, GB
linkedin_org_description:
CF is a leading consultancy dedicated to making an enduring impact on health and healthcare. We work with leaders and frontline teams to improve health, transform healthcare, embed life science innovation and boost growth through investment.

With unmatched access to UK healthcare data and award-winning data science expertise, our team are a driving force for delivering positive and meaningful change.

title: Alpha Head of Global Fund Development Events (Maternity Cover)
description_text:
Alpha is seeking an experienced and creative events leader to shape and deliver exceptional global donor experiences. This role is central to the planning and delivery of high‑profile events for Alpha’s global partners, with a particular focus on Leadership Conference and other key donor events.
You will provide strategic leadership for Alpha’s donor events, working closely with senior leaders, global teams, and key stakeholders to steward and grow relationships with high‑net‑worth individuals, trusts, foundations, and organisations.
Key Responsibilities
Support delivery of the Leadership Conference 2026 partner experience and lead vision, planning, and delivery for Leadership Conference 2027.
Plan and deliver exclusive, high‑value donor events, including hospitality, seating, private functions, and guest experience design.
Lead Alpha’s partner-facing events at Focus 2026, in collaboration with the CEO and President’s offices.
Line manage the Fund Development Events Manager and lead event staff, volunteers, and contractors.
Manage suppliers, production, logistics, and on‑site delivery.
Collaborate with data, marketing, and regional teams to manage guest journeys, communications, and compliance.
This is a unique opportunity to shape world‑class events that advance Alpha’s mission and global fundraising strategy.
Committed to the vision of Alpha & HTB Group
Proven leadership in delivering large, high‑profile events
Experience working with high‑net‑worth individuals and senior stakeholders
Strong strategic thinking with the ability to deliver complex, multi‑stakeholder projects
Calm, proactive self‑starter who performs well under pressure
We offer a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your wellbeing and work-life balance, including:
25 days annual leave (plus public holidays) , which increases with length of service
Pension scheme contributions
Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
Medicash plan
Enhanced maternity, paternity, adoption, and shared parental leave
Access to training and development resources to help you grow in your role
This role will remain open for 30 days, however we may close the advert earlier if we find the right candidate
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves planning and delivering high-profile donor events, particularly the Leadership Conference, and managing the overall partner experience. Additionally, the position includes line management of the Fund Development Events Manager and collaboration with various teams to ensure successful event execution.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have proven leadership experience in delivering large events and a strong ability to work with high-net-worth individuals and senior stakeholders. Strategic thinking and the capacity to manage complex projects are essential, along with a proactive and calm demeanor under pressure.
linkedin_org_locations: An der Reeperbahn 4a, Bremen, HB 28217, DE
linkedin_org_description:
Die HTB Group mit Sitz in Bremen ist ein bankenunabhängiges Unternehmen, spezialisiert auf Alternative Investment Fonds (AIF) und Immobilien-Asset-Management.

Die Service-Kapitalverwaltungsgesellschaft (SKVG) als Dienstleister ist ein spezialisiertes Unternehmen, das Initiatoren bei der Prospektierung und dem laufenden Geschäftsbetrieb nach KAGB unterstützt.

Privatanleger, professionelle sowie institutionelle Anleger finden bei der HTB Group attraktive Anlageprodukte und maßgeschneiderte Lösungen.

title: Middle Scala Developer
description_text:
At BetSymphony, we are embarking on an ambitious project to revolutionize the online gambling industry. Our vision is to develop a cutting-edge gambling platform that will serve as the backbone for multiple different brands, each with its unique identity and target audience. This platform will leverage the latest technologies to ensure a seamless, secure, and engaging experience for users across the globe. 
As a Middle Scala Developer on our team, you will play a pivotal role in bringing this vision to life. You will be involved in every stage of the development process, from initial design to deployment, focusing on creating a scalable, high-performance backend system that can support the diverse needs of multiple brands. 
Working on this project will expose you to the latest technologies and challenges in software development, offering ample opportunities for growth and innovation. You will be part of a dynamic team that values creativity, collaboration, and a commitment to excellence.
At least 3 years of experience building backend systems.
At least 2 years of experience working with Scala.
Experience building RESTful APIs / WebSockets.
Proficient in Scala and its ecosystem (e.g., Akka, Play Framework, SBT).
Experience working with distributed messaging systems such as Kafka, ActiveMQ, RabbitMQ, etc.
Experience with microservices architecture.
Containerisation technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes).
Strong understanding of software design patterns, data structures, and algorithms.
Experience with database technologies (SQL and NoSQL).
Familiarity with cloud services (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and DevOps tools (CI/CD pipelines).
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English (B2)
Be able to work in multicultural work environment.
Strong problem-solving skills.
Self-organized and able to quickly learn new technologies and paradigms.
Desirable:
Experience with JIRA, and Confluence.
Experience with ZIO and/or Typelevel stack (CATS etc.)
Experience with Couchbase DB
Experience with ElasticSearch
Experience in gambling or online gaming.
Experience with TDD development processes. 
Strong interest in applying AI-powered tools (e.g. ChatGPT, Copilot, Cursor or similar) to speed up day-to-day development tasks.
Proven ability or strong motivation to use AI to reduce development time, improve code quality, and automate repetitive engineering work.
Experience in leveraging AI for code generation, refactoring, debugging, test creation, and documentation.
Openness to experimenting with new AI tools and workflows and integrating them into the development process.
Responsibilities:
New features development within existing product.
Communicating with team members and Product team.
Resolving technical problems as they rise.
Bug fixing of newly developed features (no refactoring of the old code).
Writing Unit tests and integration tests.
Scrum ceremonies participation.
Mentoring middle and junior colleagues might be required.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves developing new features within the existing product and resolving technical problems as they arise. Additionally, the developer will participate in Scrum ceremonies and may mentor junior colleagues.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have at least 3 years of experience in building backend systems and 2 years of experience working with Scala. Proficiency in Scala and its ecosystem, as well as experience with distributed messaging systems and microservices architecture, is required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Write Code Like Music
linkedin_org_locations: Laarderhoogtweg 25, 1101 EB, Amsterdam, North Holland 1101 EB, NL
linkedin_org_description:
Symphony Solutions is a Cloud and AI-driven IT company headquartered in the Netherlands. We are a premier software provider of custom iGaming, Healthcare, and Airline solutions. Devoted to delivering the highest quality of service, we offer our expertise in full-cycle software development, cloud engineering, data and analytics, AI services, digital marketing orchestration, and more. Since our founding in 2008, Symphony Solutions has been serving more than 35 international clients primarily in Western Europe and North America.  

Central to our approach is nurturing strong relationships with clients and employees and cultivating an atmosphere of intimacy. We bring the spirit of freedom, individuality, and innovation into the tech world and build a virtual community for IT professionals to thrive and work on state-of-art solutions that bring real value to our clients.  

The Foundation of Our Culture:  
- We are mastering AI tools to bring out the best of our potential and capabilities.  
- We create a diverse environment where individuals of all backgrounds and identities grow personally and professionally.  
- We build relationships to last and thrive, surrounded by freedom, support, and creativity.  
- We nurture vibrant virtual communities that connect people across tech and non-tech interest groups.  
- We go beyond the traditional office and work from Anywhere.   
- We give to those in need and strive to make this world a better place for all.  

From startups to Fortune 500 companies, we have a proven track record of delivering exceptional results for our clients. With a focus on innovation, quality, and reliability, we're committed to helping businesses of all sizes achieve success in today's digital world.

title: Software Engineer in Test IV
description_text:
TCP is committed to cultivating a diverse and inclusive team. However, we are not able to sponsor visas for this role. 
About TCP (TimeClock Plus):
For more than 30 years, TCP has helped organizations engage their people by providing flexible, mobile timekeeping and workforce management solutions. Trusted by tens of thousands of customers and millions of users, TCP delivers best-in-class technology and personalized support to organizations of all sizes in the public and private sector to meet their complex timekeeping, employee scheduling, leave management and other workforce needs. Growth is happening and our vision for a successful future is clear - We'd love for you to join us on this journey! For more information on TCP, visit www.tcpsoftware.com or follow us on LinkedIn or Facebook. 
We are seeking a Senior Mobile QA Engineer with 5+ years of hands-on experience in both manual and automation testing. You will independently drive quality for our mobile products, influence team standards, and share your expertise to elevate others.
Key Responsibilities:
Own end-to-end mobile QA for iOS, Android, or other platforms—manual and automated.
Design and execute robust test cases and automation suites.
Lead by example: impact team quality, share knowledge, and foster best practices.
Automate testing for multiple mobile apps (not just a few).
Report defects, analyze results, and recommend improvements.
Ensure compliance with product, technical, and regulatory standards.
Requirements:
5+ years in mobile QA (manual & automation).
Proven automation experience: built/maintained test frameworks and automated more than a few mobile apps.
Proficient in tools like Appium, Espresso, XCUITest, or similar.
Strong Java (OOP) and experience with GIT/Jenkins.
Solid API & UI testing skills; experience with cloud (AWS) environments.
Independent, open-minded, collaborative, and able to influence and compromise.
Excellent communication and organizational skills.
Full remote work, with the option to work from the office, based on personal preference.
Personalised and individual benefits plan. 
Competitive salary based on experience.
Voluntary Health insurance plan. 
International working environment.
8 hours to volunteer and impact the community.
The work/life setup you need to be successful.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves owning end-to-end mobile QA for iOS, Android, or other platforms, including both manual and automated testing. The engineer will design and execute robust test cases, lead by example, and ensure compliance with various standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 5+ years of experience in mobile QA, with proven automation experience and proficiency in tools like Appium and Espresso. Strong skills in Java, API and UI testing, and experience with cloud environments are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Learn how TCP Software can help your team work better.
linkedin_org_description: For 35 years, TCP Software has helped organizations engage their people by providing flexible workforce management solutions and mobile timekeeping. Trusted by 30,000 customers and millions of users, TCP delivers best-in-class technology and support to organizations of all sizes in the public and private sector to meet their complex employee scheduling, leave management, timekeeping and other workforce needs.

title: Cybersecurity & Data Security Junior Associate (Development Focus, UK Scope)
description_text:
At peopleworth, we support work where people and performance thrive. As part of our Employer Group, we work with a variety of forward-thinking partners and are excited to share this opportunity that sits within our growing group.
Role Overview
This is a structured development fixed-term opportunity for an early-career professional eager to gain experience in cybersecurity, data protection, and risk management within a UK focussed context, while being based in the Western Cape. As a Cybersecurity & Data Security Junior Associate, you’ll participate in real-world projects that strengthen organisations’ defences against data risks, system vulnerabilities, and compliance breaches. The role blends guided learning with meaningful, independent contributions to advisory, audit, and awareness initiatives.
Key Responsibilities
Participate in cybersecurity risk assessments and assist in documenting findings and recommendations 
Contribute to the development and review of information-security policies and compliance procedures 
Support internal audit and governance activities related to data protection and cyber-risk management 
Monitor and report on emerging threats such as phishing, spam, and data breaches 
Assist with preparing client and internal training materials on cybersecurity awareness and best practices 
Research global cybersecurity standards (ISO 27001, NIST, POPIA, GDPR) and help translate them into practical insights 
Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams across time zones to deliver high-quality advisory outputs 
Take ownership of specific deliverables while engaging in continuous learning and skills development 
Based in the Western Cape (around Cape Town, Stellenbosch, Strand or Paarl areas for occasional travel to group offices, while mainly working remotely) 
Foundational understanding of cybersecurity, data privacy, or risk management concepts 
Exposure to international data-protection frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST, POPIA, or GDPR) is advantageous 
Keen interest in advisory and policy work related to data protection and system security 
Strong research, analytical, and communication skills 
Self-motivated with the ability to manage tasks independently while contributing to team objectives 
Bachelor’s degree, diploma, or ongoing studies in Cybersecurity, Information Systems, Data Management, Legal, Risk, or a related field 
Genuine curiosity about emerging cyber threats and an eagerness to grow through applied project experience 
A people-centred, collaborative environment focused on growth and learning 
Real-world exposure to global cybersecurity and data-compliance challenges 
Structured mentoring and development opportunities without rigid supervision 
Scope to build your career into a professional cybersecurity consulting pathway 
Our Recruitment Process
The peopleworth Employer Group follows a fair, transparent, and multi-stage recruitment process designed to ensure mutual fit.
Application Submission: Complete the online form and answer brief application questions. 
Initial Screening: Your application is reviewed for role alignment; successful candidates move to the longlist. 
Video Interview Stage: You’ll be invited to record short responses to 3–4 role-specific questions. 
Live Interviews: Shortlisted candidates join first-round interviews (and, where applicable, second or third rounds depending on the role). 
Final Shortlist & Verification: Reference and background checks are completed. 
Offer & Contracting: Successful candidates receive formal offers and contract documents. 
Pre-boarding & Onboarding: Once accepted, you’ll complete a pre-boarding process before officially joining your employing organisation within the Employer Group.
ai_remote_location_derived: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves participating in cybersecurity risk assessments, documenting findings, and contributing to the development of information-security policies. Additionally, the associate will support internal audit activities and monitor emerging threats.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a foundational understanding of cybersecurity and data privacy concepts, with a keen interest in advisory and policy work. A bachelor's degree or ongoing studies in relevant fields is preferred.
linkedin_org_description:
Sometimes your business can have the best product in the world. You can have some of the brightest minds. But success or, as is more often the case, failure comes down to one thing. People. 

A high performing, well-engaged workforce, with a set of robust processes that enable them to maximise performance in their job roles, while protecting you as an employer, doesn’t just appear from thin air and miraculously work in harmony.

Peopleworth covers every employee milestone and touchpoint, and demonstrates the positive commercial impact of putting people first in your organisation. 

The net result? Business performance, business culture, business integrity.

Our Services:
Strategic HR
Culture and employee engagement
Recruitment branding and employer value proposition
Communications

title: Βοηθός Λογιστή (1 θέση) - Υπάλληλος Γραφείου (1 θέση)
description_text:
Βοηθός Λογιστή (1 θέση)
Προσόντα 
Απόφοιτος ΑΕΙ/ΤΕΙ Λογιστικής,Οκονομικής ή Χρηματοοικονομικής κατεύθυνσης
Επιθυμητή προϋπηρεσία σε τεχνική εταιρεία
Εξοικείωση με λογιστικά προγράμματα/ ERP και My Data
Συμφωνίες λογαριασμών,υπολοίπων τραπεζών,πελατών και προμηθευτών.
Γνώση χειρισμού εφαρμογών MS Office (ιδίως Excel) 
Ομαδικό πνεύμα και επαγγελματισμός
Εκπληρωμένες στρατιωτικές υποχρεώσεις
Υπάλληλος Γραφείου (Διαχείρισης-Παρακολούθησης Εταιρικού Στόλου) (1 θέση)
Αρμοδιότητες:
Ευθύνη για την ασφαλή και νόμιμη λειτουργία των οχημάτων του στόλου (ασφάλιση, διαχείριση δηλώσεων ατυχημάτων, τέλη κυκλοφορίας, ΚΤΕΟ, ΚΕΚ, πιστοποιηικά ταχογράφων κλπ.) 
Διαχείριση και παρακολούθηση των πομποδεκτών διοδίων των οχημάτων του στόλου
Διαχείριση και παρακολούθηση των καρτών καυσίμων των οχημάτων του στόλου
Διαχείριση και παρακολούθηση GPS στόλου (fleet management)
Συνεργασία με προσωπικό αποθήκης-συνεργείου της εταιρείας για τον προγραμματισμό της τακτικής συντήρησης (service, ελαστικά) των οχημάτων του στόλου όπως και για την αποκατάσταση βλαβών σε συνεργαζόμενα συνεργεία
Έλεγχος κατανάλωσης καυσίμων των οχημάτων του στόλου 
Δημιουργία αναφορών για το κόστος των συντηρήσεων και το κόστος κατανάλωσης καυσίμων των οχημάτων του στόλου
Προσόντα
Πτυχίο ΤΕΙ ή ΙΕΚ με ειδικότητα Οχημάτων είτε Μηχανολόγου ή Ηλεκτρολόγου Μηχανικού
Επιθυμητή προϋπηρεσία σε τεχνική εταιρεία
Γνώση χειρισμού Η/Υ, MS-Office και ERP 
Καλή γνώση Αγγλικών
Εργατικότητα και ομαδικό πνεύμα
Εκπληρωμένες στρατιωτικές υποχρεώσεις
Μετά τη συλλογή και αξιολόγηση των βιογραφικών σημειωμάτων θα επικοινωνήσουμε με τους υποψηφίους που ανταποκρίνονται στις απαιτήσεις της θέσης προς στελέχωση για να οριστεί συνάντηση προς συνέντευξη.
Οι ενδιαφερόμενοι που πληρούν τις παραπάνω προϋποθέσεις, παρακαλούνται να αποστείλουν βιογραφικό σημείωμα στην ηλεκτρονική διεύθυνση hr@odos-tsigos.gr
Όλες οι αιτήσεις θα θεωρηθούν απόλυτα εμπιστευτικές.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Assistant Accountant is responsible for account reconciliations and managing financial records, while the Office Employee oversees the safe and legal operation of the company's vehicle fleet. This includes managing insurance, accident reports, and maintenance schedules.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field for the Assistant Accountant position, and a degree in Vehicle Engineering or Mechanical/Electrical Engineering for the Office Employee position. Previous experience in a technical company and proficiency in relevant software are preferred.

title: Waiter/ Waitress- Westfield, White City, London
description_text:
TOCA Social is the world's first dining and entertainment experience with a football twist, and it’s set to change the game! Our first venue opened in 2021 in The O2, London and our second in Birmingham in 2024. We are also set to open in Westfield London, Dallas and Mexico in 2025! 
At TOCA Social, Everybody Plays! Football knowledge is not required, so if you thrive being in an energetic and fun environment you’ll quickly fall in love with our concept!
Benefits:
You’ll have full access to our TOCA Treats, which includes (but is not limited to!): 
£12.21 per hour plus service charge! (Up to £20 per hour OTE)
Complimentary TOCA games!
Huge discounts on our F&B 
Free meal whilst on shift
Local retail & hospitality discounts!
Teammate Assistance Programme with access to free counselling sessions
Great progression opportunities as we expand!
Job Highlights:
Location: TOCA Social, Westfield White City, London, W12 7GF
Reports to: Hospitality Manager
Hours Required: Part Time and Full Time roles available. Candidates must be flexible to work evenings and weekends.
The dynamic role of a Hospitality Champion means no 2 days are the same at TOCA Social! You are responsible for bringing together multiple elements of our guests' journey together to deliver memorable experiences! We’re passionate about training and developing all our teammates to support their career progression and help them become legends, so you may receive training within other departments to support the venue as and when necessary!
Role Scope & Responsibilities:
With a can-do attitude, we’ll provide you with the tools and training you need to be a Hospitality Champion which involves: 
Greeting & supporting guests through their journey to provide a best in class experience
Taking guest orders, delivering food and drinks and checking in with guests 
Conducting box demo’s and delivering steps of service to the TOCA Social standards
Handling guest enquiries and bookings in person and via phone and upselling where possible
Managing our cloakroom and boot room as needed 
Walking guests through our F&B offering and making recommendations 
Having an eye for opportunity to exceed expectations in a positive, surprising and delightful manner 
Ensuring guest facing areas are clean and presentable at all times
Assisting guests with transactions in an efficient & timely manner, being accountable for all transactions 
Proving clear and concise communication to teammates to minimise disruption to guests 
Ensuring all items/stock are replenished and available to guests 
Supporting kitchen and bar teams when necessary e.g. preparing food, making cocktails etc - you’ll be a ‘jack of all trades!’ 
Following all health & safety and allergy procedures 
Providing basic tech troubleshooting 
Being flexible in covering a range of duties in line with business needs when required
Qualifications & Experience:
A punctual and reliable individual with a great attitude and work ethic! 
A quick learner with the ability to work under pressure in a fast paced environment
Passionate about delivering top quality experiences 
A willingness and desire to make people happy and deliver genuine and memorable experiences
Someone who is ready to roll up their sleeves and get stuck in (and help out other departments and teammates!)
ai_benefits: Teammate Assistance Programme With Access To Free Counselling Sessions
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves greeting and supporting guests, taking orders, delivering food and drinks, and ensuring a clean and presentable environment. Additionally, the waiter/waitress will assist with guest inquiries and support kitchen and bar teams as needed.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should be punctual, reliable, and possess a great attitude and work ethic. A willingness to learn and a passion for delivering quality experiences are essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Are you game?
linkedin_org_locations: TOCA, Third Floor, 240 High Holborn, London, UK WC1V 7DN, GB
linkedin_org_description:
TOCA Social is the new entertainment concept transforming how we meet and play. Built around accessible and interactive football-based games, our stylish venues give players of all kinds a place to come together and put their name in lights.

With live events, a menu to wow and an array of world-class cocktails, football’s never looked so good.

At TOCA Social, everybody plays.

title: Property consultant - Real estate
description_text:
Role Description
This is a full-time, on-site role for a Real Estate Broker located in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The Real Estate Broker will be responsible for assisting clients in buying, selling, or renting properties. Daily tasks include conducting market analyses, providing client consultations, preparing property listings, negotiating contracts, and ensuring excellent customer service. The role requires collaboration with internal teams and external stakeholders to achieve client objectives and close successful transactions.
Role & Responsibilities 
Generate and follow up on leads, build a strong client database, and identify new business opportunities.
Conduct property viewings, site visits, and client presentations.
Advise clients on market conditions, property values, legal requirements, and investment opportunities.
Negotiate property prices, terms, and agreements between buyers, sellers, landlords, and tenants.
Prepare, verify, and coordinate all real estate documentation, including offers, contracts, and tenancy agreements.
Ensure timely coordination of transactions through to successful closing and handover.
Maintain accurate records of listings, clients, and transactions in company systems.
Achieve assigned sales targets and performance objectives.
Ensure full compliance with UAE real estate laws, RERA guidelines, and company policies.
Build and maintain professional relationships with property owners, developers, and industry partners.
Represent the company in a professional manner and uphold its brand and reputation at all times.
Qualification & Experience
Bachelors would be an additional advantage
1 year of UAE Real Estate Market experience
Package:
Commission 50% on closing deals
No Basic Salary
Exclusive Leads – Verified, high-quality leads to support your closing success
Career Progression – Clear pathway to team leadership and management roles based on performance
Visa & Broker Card – Full compliance support for seamless operation in the Dubai market
Training & Development – Continuous learning, leadership coaching, and senior mentorship
Marketing Power – Access to premium tools and targeted campaigns to amplify your listings
ai_core_responsibilities: The Real Estate Broker will assist clients in buying, selling, or renting properties, conducting market analyses, and ensuring excellent customer service. They will also negotiate contracts and collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders to achieve client objectives.

title: Driver/Warehouse Operative - Renewable Energy - Livingston, Scotland
description_text:
With the UK government’s ambitious goal of delivering 60GW of solar power by 2030, Solar Careers UK are on a mission to help recruit, train, and place over 43,000 new professionals into rewarding careers within the solar and battery storage industry.
Solar Careers UK is a division of Solar Energy UK, the trade association representing over 425 solar businesses across the country. Many of these companies specialise in delivering high-quality solar installations for domestic, commercial, and utility-scale projects.
For the industry's most in-demand jobs, we have been asked to assist with the recruitment of full-time teams from across all regions of the United Kingdom.
By completing this application Solar Careers UK can help connect you to Driver/Warehouse Operative live job vacancies that match your skills and experience, and provide guidance on the qualifications and training needed to pursue your chosen career in the solar sector.
New entrants welcome.
Ready to drive your career forward in one of the fastest-growing industries of our time? Join a pioneering business in the wholesale renewable energy sector, leading the way in solar panels and alternative energy solutions.
We’re on the lookout for a proactive Driver/Warehouse Operative to join their wholesale site in Livingston, Scotland. This is a fantastic chance to be part of a team making real impact in a sector that’s shaping the future.
Why This Role Stands Out
This isn’t just another warehouse role. You’ll have variety every day, switching between keeping operations in the warehouse running smoothly and heading out on the road to deliver and collect. Think of it as the best of both worlds: hands-on logistics plus the freedom of driving.
What You’ll Be Doing
Taking charge of incoming deliveries, checking manifests and spotting any damage
Packing and processing orders with care, ready for courier dispatch
Hitting the road in the company van for supplier collections and customer deliveries
Keeping the warehouse tidy, safe, and well-stocked
Supporting customers with collections and vehicle loading
Pitching in with all aspects of warehouse life — no two days are the same
Building solid relationships with customers and colleagues alike
What We’re Looking For
A full driving licence (no more than 6 points/no endorsements)
Experience in renewable energy/solar (a bonus, not a deal-breaker) 
Wholesale experience is preferable
Confident IT and admin skills
Great communicator who thrives in a busy environment
Positive, flexible, can-do attitude
What’s In It For You
Competitive pay (£27–28K DOE)
Company pension scheme
Free on-site parking
Monday–Friday day shift
ai_core_responsibilities: The Driver/Warehouse Operative will manage incoming deliveries, process orders, and ensure the warehouse is organized and safe. The role also includes driving for supplier collections and customer deliveries.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should possess a full driving licence and have experience in wholesale, with renewable energy experience being a bonus. Strong IT skills and effective communication are essential for success in this busy environment.
linkedin_org_slogan: Catalysing the collective strengths of our members to build a clean energy system for everyone’s benefit.
linkedin_org_locations: 6 Langley Street, London, England WC2H 9JA, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Solar Energy UK represents over 400+ member companies operating in the UK energy sector and beyond.

Solar energy's exceptional synergies with energy storage, electric vehicles and smart grids means the industry works on the frontline of technology and system change to deliver net zero carbon emissions. Our incisive research, policy development and influence shapes policy and regulation, and drives market growth. 

In partnership with key players across the value-chain, Solar Energy UK is working to five-fold increase the UK solar capacity to 70GW by 2035.

description_text:
Job Title: Service Desk Engineer (End User Support)
Location: Based from our Newcastle office (Cobalt Business Park)
Annual salary: Circa £24,000 depending on skills & experience
About Cybit
Predict. Protect. Perform
Cybit delivers data and AI‑led technology, to help organisations work more securely, productively, and with confidence across digital workspaces, cloud, security, and managed services. Our mission is to create positive business outcomes and support the people and communities our customers serve. We believe the right technology, delivered well, drives resilience, performance, and meaningful impact.
Why Join Us?
Be part of a dynamic, outcome‑focused, and people‑first team across Berkshire and Newcastle, where talent, ideas, and collaboration are genuinely valued. You’ll have the opportunity to make a real impact on how we deliver technology and how our brand is experienced, while building a meaningful career and helping to create a safer, more resilient future.
Guide to what a Service Desk Engineer (End User Support) does at Cybit
Working within the End User Support team, the Service Desk Engineer provides technical support and maintenance to all supported IT business users, systems, hardware, and applications. Whilst providing expert technical support, the Service Desk Engineer will ensure incidents and requests are resolved in an efficient and professional manner in line with service level commitments.
This role offers clear opportunities for progression and professional development, providing a strong pathway to grow capability and advance your career within a dynamic technology organisation.
We are looking for someone who
Is enthusiastic, reliable, and keen to learn and progress in IT
Has knowledge of Windows 10/11 and common desktop hardware (laptops, PCs, printers) 
Has exposure to Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive) or a willingness to learn 
Understands IT fundamentals such as user accounts, passwords, and permissions 
Delivers excellent customer service and communicates effectively
Takes a logical approach to troubleshooting technical issues 
Is able to follow processes, document work, and update tickets clearly 
Can manage time and priorities in a busy support environment 
Maintains a positive attitude and stays calm when supporting non-technical users
Be well looked after
Happy people make happy businesses. We have a great range of flexible benefits which helps you to do your best work every day. This includes:
Enhanced Pension contribution scheme
33 days annual leave including bank holidays (& the opportunity to buy more if desired)
Healthcare cash plan
Private Medical Insurance (after 12 months service)
Company electric car scheme
Cycle to work scheme
Employee assistance programme
Occupational sick pay
Birthday day off
Enhanced family leave
Volunteering time available
Cybit is passionate about having a diverse workforce by encouraging equality, diversity, and inclusion throughout the business. We have an inclusive environment where employees are encouraged to speak openly about what matters to them. This allows us all to learn and grow together within a safe and respectful environment. We provide equal opportunities to all and are a proud member of the Disability Confident scheme.

description_text:
Initial Washroom Hygiene Service Driver
Join Our Team and Make a Difference!
We are currently seeking a Service Driver to join our dedicated team at the Newcastle branch, covering the Newcastle, NE postcode area. If you enjoy driving, managing your own schedule and providing excellent customer service, this could be the perfect opportunity for you!
Why Join Rentokil Initial?
Competitive Salary Package: Start with a basic salary of £25732 per annum.
Expected OTE: £26500 per annum, with bonus and commission schemes available.
Benefits: Company vehicle, fuel card, mobile phone, uniform, and RI Rewards.
Relocation Package: Moving from more than 2 hours away? We may offer up to £5,000 to help you settle in.
Work-Life Balance: Full-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday (40 hr week), with potential for up to 48 hours in the future with an increased salary.
Industry-Leading Training: Receive top-notch training to support our customers’ needs.
The Service Driver Role
As a driver, you will visit a number of different customer sites to service their washroom products. These products may include feminine hygiene units, nappy bins, air fresheners, soap dispensers and floor mats. Key responsibilities include: 
Loading and unloading units and floor mats for customers, so you will need to be physically fit for this role
Ensuring that your vehicle is safe at all times, driving responsibly and following our RI Drive Smart guidance
Providing exceptional customer service and upholding a professional image at all times
Service Driver Requirements:
Full UK driving licence held for more than two years, with no more than six penalty points
Demonstrate excellent customer service and communication skills
Flexibility with working patterns to support business needs
Ability to use own initiative and have a positive ‘can do’ attitude
Pride in the job you do
You may be required to pass a DBS check depending on the role you have applied for
Service Driver Benefits:
Additional opportunities to earn more with regular bonus and commission schemes.
Access to a company vehicle and fuel card.
Salary grading system - linked to performance for those keen to develop their career within our business.

Exciting discounts and cashback offers from over 3,000 retailers with RI Rewards.

Leads Commission scheme available - although this isn’t a sales job, we appreciate and reward you for passing on sales leads.
Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) - free to access and available 24/7 to you, your family, and friends.
Long service recognition - includes an extra five days of annual leave entitlement after five years of service.
Refer a Friend - earn up to £1,000 for referring someone to work for Rentokil Initial
A Company Putting “People First”
Rentokil Initial (FTSE100) is one of the largest business services companies in the world, operating in over 90 countries and providing services that protect people and enhance lives. title: Product Marketing Leader
description_text:
The opportunity
Burp Suite technology is trusted by over 18,000 organisations worldwide, including Microsoft, Amazon, and NASA. As we continue to expand our product portfolio and deepen our presence in enterprise markets, we’re investing in Product Marketing as a critical capability across Product, Sales, and Go-to-Market.
Product Marketing at PortSwigger is at an inflection point.
We have strong foundations: world-class technology, a passionate technical audience, and emerging Product Marketing capability. What we need now is experienced leadership to turn this into a coherent, durable function that creates clarity from complexity and scales with the business.
This role is not about producing more assets. It’s about defining how Product Marketing works at PortSwigger and raising the bar across positioning, launches, and cross-functional alignment.
Why this role exists
PortSwigger is a company built around a simple belief: security should empower people, not slow them down. We create world-class tooling that helps security teams and developers uncover and fix real vulnerabilities, the kind that actually matter. Our products are crafted with care, backed by deep research, and shaped by a relentless drive to make the web safer for everyone.
We’re a successful, engineering-led organisation where small squads are empowered to take forward work in a suitable way, high-leverage work. Curiosity fuels us, craftsmanship defines us, and mutual support keeps us growing together. Here, you’ll have real agency, clear direction, and the freedom to push boundaries, supported by thoughtful coaching and a culture that values learning as much as delivery.
The Growth Tribe
You’ll join the Growth Tribe, focused on helping more of the right customers discover, understand, adopt, and succeed with PortSwigger—turning deep technical value into clear outcomes across the full go-to-market journey.
What you’ll doDefine Product Marketing strategy and operating model
Establish a clear Product Marketing mandate, operating rhythm, and standards. 
Define what Product Marketing owns, influences, and supports across Product, Sales, Customer Success, and Marketing. 
Create lightweight but effective processes for intake, prioritisation, and launches. 
Drive our strategic positioning and narrative
Define and maintain clear positioning across products and capabilities. 
Create a coherent market narrative that balances technical depth with buyer clarity. 
Make hard trade-offs on messaging to avoid fragmentation as the portfolio grows. 
Lead launch strategy and execution
Set the standard for confident, coordinated product and feature launches. 
Partner with Product and GTM teams to align roadmap context, messaging, and timing. 
Ensure launches translate into real-world impact across sales, marketing, and customers. 
Be a commercially credible GTM partner
Work closely with Sales leadership to align positioning, pricing, packaging, and enablement. 
Use market insight and deal feedback to address objections and reduce friction in the sales cycle. 
Prioritise Product Marketing work based on business impact, not volume. 
Coach and develop Product Marketing capability
Support and develop Product Marketers in thinking, craft, and execution. 
Raise quality through feedback, shared frameworks, and reusable artefacts. 
Act as a hands-on leader: close to the work, but focused on leverage and consistency. 
About you
You are an experienced Product Marketer who has helped build Product Marketing into a respected, high-impact function. You likely:
Have led or been a senior Product Marketing hire in a B2B SaaS company, ideally with technical or developer-facing products. 
Have experience operating in ambiguity and shaping Product Marketing before everything was clearly defined. 
Are equally comfortable with strategic positioning conversations and rolling up your sleeves to write, review, and refine messaging. 
Can partner credibly with Product and Sales leaders, balancing technical depth with commercial outcomes. 
Have coached other Product Marketers and set standards that improved consistency and impact across a team.
You’re opinionated, thoughtful, and pragmatic. You care about clarity over noise, outcomes over output, and building things that last.
Why this role matters
Product Marketing is becoming increasingly central to how we:
Communicate complex technical value to diverse audiences 
Launch products and capabilities with confidence 
Align Product, Sales, and Marketing around shared context and priorities 

This role will shape how Product Marketing operates at PortSwigger for years to come.
At PortSwigger, we believe people should be paid what they’re truly worth, and we aim to make this a place where you can do the best work of your life. You’ll receive a competitive compensation package and a strong set of benefits, alongside a culture of high performance and high support, where you’ll have the autonomy to make an impact and the backing to grow.
ai_core_responsibilities: Define the Product Marketing strategy and operating model while establishing clear mandates and standards. Lead launch strategy and execution, ensuring alignment across Product, Sales, and Marketing.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate should have experience in B2B SaaS, particularly with technical or developer-facing products, and should be comfortable with both strategic and hands-on tasks. They should also have a track record of coaching Product Marketers and improving team standards.

title: Crew Assistant
description_text:
Job Description
Job Title: Crew Assistant
Reports to: Crew Manager
Location: Kifisia - GR 
Job Summary: The Crew Assistant supports the Crew Department in the effective planning, coordination, and administration of vessel manning operations. The role ensures that all crew-related activities are carried out in compliance with company policies, flag state regulations, and international maritime standards, contributing to the safe and efficient operation of the fleet.
About the company
We are a growth-oriented global shipping company engaged in the seaborne transportation of a wide range of commodities along worldwide shipping routes through our ownership of dry bulk and tanker vessels. Our primary goas is to grow our fleet through acquisitions of new and modern vessels. 
Main Responsibilities
Assist in the coordination of crew planning, reliefs, and rotations for assigned vessels 
Maintain accurate and up to date records, certificates and documentation
Support the recruitment process, including screening CVs and liaising with manning agents 
Coordinate crew travel arrangements, visas and embarkation/disembarkation logistics 
Monitor crew certification, medicals, and training requirements ensuring compliance 
Assist with crew related cost control 
Support audits, inspections and internal reporting related to crewing activities 
Desired Skills & Experience 
Educated to degree level, preferably in Maritime Studies, Shipping or related field 
1-3 years of experience in crewing or maritime operations 
Familiarity with crew management processes
Experience in coordinating crew documentation, certification, medicals and travel arrangements 
Strong organizational and administrative skills with high attention to detail 
Effective communication and interpersonal skills 
Computer literate (Microsoft Office 365, BENEFIT ERP system)
Ability to work under pressure, manage multiple tasks, and meet tight deadlines
Proactive, positive working attitude with strong sense of responsibility and teamwork 
Fluent in both Greek and English (any other languages is a plus)
Life & Health Insurance 
Dental Insurance 
Daily Lunch Allowance 
Special Bday Gift 
Discretionary Annual Bonus 
Parking 
Opportunities for Growth
ai_core_responsibilities: The Crew Assistant coordinates crew planning, reliefs, and rotations while maintaining accurate records and documentation. They also support recruitment processes and ensure compliance with crew certification and training requirements.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a degree in Maritime Studies or a related field and 1-3 years of experience in crewing or maritime operations. Strong organizational skills and fluency in Greek and English are also required.
linkedin_org_description:
CASTOR SHIPS S.A. is a technical and commercial ship management company established in Greece in 2020 under law 27/75.
The mission of CASTOR SHIPS S.A. is to operate a continuously mixed growing fleet of Oil Tankers, LPGs, Bulk Carriers and Containers in the most effective, efficient and the safest way, serving the needs of our customers, protecting their interests and fulfilling their expectations, showing a commitment to continuous improvement on Safety and ESG matters.
Our vision is to become the leaders in our field through continuous development of business excellence and sustainability aspects, by treating the social concerns of the shipping industry in a responsible manner, embracing innovation and new technologies and promoting ethical business conduct.

Join Our Team and Make a Difference!

title: Executive Assistant
description_text:
CommonAI is redefining the way we develop deep tech AI solutions through common infrastructure and collaborative engineering. CommonAI Compute is part of the CommonAI group, delivering cost effective multi-cloud compute services for AI teams. This is an opportunity to join a fast-growing company in AI and cloud computing that is bringing together commercial investment, government and research to ensure that we maximise success of AI across the UK and Europe.
In this busy and varied role you will be based at our Cambridge office providing high-level administrative and operational support to CommonAI Compute’s senior leadership group. This role requires exceptional organisational skills, discretion, and the ability to anticipate needs in a fast-paced environment. You will act as a trusted partner to the CTO and work alongside CommonAI CICs Executive Assistant ensuring priorities are managed efficiently and communications run smoothly. Specific responsibilities will include:
Executive Support - diary management, scheduling meetings, preparing agendas, reviewing presentations, reports and briefing materials, processing expenses
Operational & Administrative Support - general office management including purchasing, health & safety compliance and assistance with new joiner set-up, managing online HR systems and staff holiday approvals
Communication & Coordination - liaison with internal teams, external partners, customers and stakeholders, handling legal and technical documents
Process Improvement - identifying opportunities to streamline workflows and support with special projects and initiatives as needed
Events and hosting - arranging team building events, customer meetings, dinners, and outreach events
You will need:
Proven experience as an Executive Assistant, Personal Assistant, or similar role
Exceptional organisational and time-management skills
Strong written and verbal communication abilities, with good attention to detail
Proficiency in Google Workspace / Microsoft Office or similar office tools
An ability to problem-solve autonomously and manage multiple priorities
An enthusiastic and proactive attitude
A high level of professionalism, integrity, and confidentiality
This is a full-time role, but we are open to some flexibility, depending on candidate circumstances.
Please note that this role is working for CommonAI Compute and by applying you agree to CommonAI CIC sharing your details with them.
Competitive salary package based on experience
Professional development opportunities
A vibrant office environment located a few minutes’ walk away from Cambridge railway station with great perks including snacks and a well-equipped gym, free to office members
CommonAI Compute is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace.

We are looking for a Sales Manager to strengthen our ambitious sales teams in Almaty, Kazakhstan. You will work with the leading networking solutions in the world and master your B2B sales skills.
Teltonika’s networking solutions business unit develops and manufactures industrial and enterprise connectivity equipment, primarily routers, switches, gateways, and modems. Our networking devices serve clients in the most complex areas of Industry 4.0, Smart City, and Green Energy.
Teltonika provides an opportunity for every ambitious sales representative to improve their sales skills, develop comprehensive knowledge of products, and generate considerable earnings.
In this role, you will:
Contribute to market research, including identifying market potential and finding growth opportunities.
Build and maintain long-term relationships with new and existing customers through daily contact, presentations, and frequent visits.
Prepare commercial offers and ensure contracts to achieve assigned sales goals.
Attend exhibitions/meetings abroad to represent Teltonika Networks and its products with solutions.
Collaborate with the technical support department and product specialists to address customer requirements.
What we expect of you:
Good English language skills (both spoken and written).
Fluent Russian language skills (both spoken and written), as it will be your primary working languages.
Strong motivation to work in B2B sales (sales experience will be considered an advantage).
Organized, results-oriented and challenge-driven personality.
Ambition to generate substantial earnings by conquering foreign markets with an innovative product.
Willingness to travel abroad.
What we offer:
Great opportunities to grow in a strong and rapidly expanding company.
Interesting and ambitious projects of industrial Networking Devices.
Place where curiosity is key: here, we share knowledge and learn in training courses, conferences or business trips.
Dynamic, challenging work environment and cooperation-based internal culture.
Company-paid sports activities (basketball, football, volleyball, yoga) and other sports events.
Celebrating achievements and having fun together in team building, summer festivals, and other important occasions.
Opportunity for solid bonuses when participating in the recommendation program.
We delight with birthday and Christmas gifts and rewards on work anniversaries. 
Office snacks.
Salary
We appreciate everyone's efforts and experiences, so we offer an attractive salary matching your skillset and field expertise. The salary for this position depending on your experience and expertise, with the option to get bonuses.
If you have questions, do not hesitate to contact, Adel Raiymbekova, +7 708 813 0681, email adel.raiymbekova@teltonika-iot.net

title: Trainee Pest Control Technician
description_text:
Rentokil Pest Control Technician
Join Our Team and Make a Difference!
We are currently seeking a Pest Control Technician to join our dedicated team at the Belfast branch, covering 
the Belfast City Centre. If you enjoy managing your own schedule and solving unique customer problems in person, this could be the perfect opportunity for you!
Why Join Rentokil?
Competitive Salary Package: Start with a basic salary of £26,733 per annum.
Expected OTE: £31,000 per annum, with bonus and commission schemes available.
Benefits: Company vehicle, fuel card, mobile phone, uniform, and RI Rewards.
Relocation Package: Moving from more than 2 hours away? We may offer up to £5,000 to help you settle in.
Work-Life Balance: Full-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday (40 hr week), with potential for up to 48 hours in the future with an increased salary.
Industry-Leading Training: Receive top-notch training to support our customers’ pest control needs.
The Role
Your role as a Pest Control Technician will involve covering a dedicated geographical area and providing support to our customers by resolving their pest issues. Key responsibilities include: 
Inspecting customer premises for signs of pests and identifying potential risks.
Swiftly tackling pest problems by cleaning and removing evidence of pest behaviour.
Offering valuable advice to customers on the maintenance and hygiene of their premises.
Completing accurate reports after every visit.
Providing exceptional customer service and upholding a professional image at all times.
Full UK driving licence held for more than two years, with no more than six penalty points.
Educated to GCSE (or equivalent) in Maths and English at level C (Grade 4) or above is advantageous.
Demonstrate excellent customer service and communication skills.
Flexibility with working patterns to support business needs.
You may be required to pass a DBS check depending on the role you have applied for.
Additional opportunities to earn more with regular bonus and commission schemes.
Access to a company vehicle and fuel card.
Salary grading system - linked to performance for those keen to develop their career within our business.
Opportunity to contribute to a Private Healthcare scheme.
Enrolment in our company pension scheme.
Exciting discounts and cashback offers from over 3,000 retailers with RI Rewards.
Overtime opportunities - potential to earn extra in overtime to meet the needs of our customers.
Leads Commission scheme available - although this isn’t a sales job, we appreciate and reward you for passing on sales leads.
Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) - free to access and available 24/7 to you, your family, and friends.
Long service recognition - includes an extra five days of annual leave entitlement after five years of service.
Refer a Friend - earn up to £1,000 for referring someone to work for Rentokil Initial.
A Company Putting “People First”
Rentokil Initial (FTSE100) is one of the largest business services companies in the world, operating in over 90 countries and providing services that protect people and enhance lives. Rentokil is the world’s leading commercial pest control services provider, Initial is the world’s leading commercial hygiene services provider, and Ambius is the world’s leading provider of plants and scenting.
As a business, we focus on the Right People, doing the Right Things, and in the Right Way. We invest a lot of time and money in training and developing all our colleagues to be the best they can be, and we are always looking for talented and driven people to join our Rentokil Initial Family.
Our Social Links
Website: https://www.rentokil.co.uk/
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/rentokil-pest-control-united-kingdom
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rentokilpestcontroluk/
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/rentokil_uk
Rentokil Initial are an equal opportunities employer and are committed to creating a diverse working environment. To find out how we process your data view our careers privacy policy here

Rentokil Initial plc employs c.68,500 people across 89 countries - offering the experience and expertise of a multi-national organisation, whilst delivering services with the agility and characteristics of a local business. 

As world leaders in Pest Control and Hygiene & Well-being services, we deliver services that protect people and enhance lives, to commercial and private customers worldwide.
